issue_title: Obscurity due to sharing same config file with gunicorn
issue_body:
The same app config module is used both for the app itself and for Gunicorn.
But while `muffin.Application` only takes uppercase values, Gunicorn prefers lowercase ones.
As a result, if I set some temporary variable in the file, thinking that it will never be accessed, I can get Gunicorn reading it and using its value for something unexpected.
For example, if I have a variable named `user` then Gunicorn will try to switch worker processes to that user - and probably fail, if such user doesn't exist or if app is run as non-root user (which is usually the case).
The worst thing is that Gunicorn doesn't clearly define what exactly happened. In my case, I got the following output after starting app:
```
$ muffin app run --port 12345
[2016-07-11 15:47:06 +0000] [3] [INFO] Application is loaded: testapp
Invalid value for user: h


Error: No such user: 'h'
<env> (M=11b47) mars@bookone p36 ~/pebble/PebWare $
```
And that's all.
The culprit for such obscure output is `gunicorn.app.base.BaseApplication.do_load_config()` function which catches any exception in `load_config()` and prints it in an obscure way:
```python
try:
    self.load_default_config()
    self.load_config()
except Exception as e:
    print("\nError: %s" % str(e), file=sys.stderr)
    sys.stderr.flush()
    sys.exit(1)
```
Probably we should request them to change this message to something more informative, or at least include a stack trace...

Also, do you think this uppercase vs lowercase difference should be documented?

issue_title: Apparent misfire (wrong extension loaded) when generating GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax
issue_body:
Short version: Requesting `EXT_texture_filter_minmax` gave me `EXT_raster_multisample` instead?

Long version:
I ran GLAD off the webservice. The comment block in glad.c was

```
/*

    OpenGL, OpenGL ES loader generated by glad 0.1.16a0 on Wed Jan 10 20:23:31 2018.

    Language/Generator: C/C++
    Specification: gl
    APIs: gl=3.3, gles2=3.0
    Profile: core
    Extensions:
        GL_ARB_texture_storage,
        GL_EXT_texture_compression_s3tc,
        GL_EXT_texture_filter_anisotropic,
        GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax,
        GL_EXT_texture_sRGB,
        GL_OVR_multiview
    Loader: False
    Local files: False
    Omit khrplatform: False

    Commandline:
        --profile="core" --api="gl=3.3,gles2=3.0" --generator="c" --spec="gl" --no-loader --extensions="GL_ARB_texture_storage,GL_EXT_texture_compression_s3tc,GL_EXT_texture_filter_anisotropic,GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax,GL_EXT_texture_sRGB,GL_OVR_multiview"
    Online:
        http://glad.dav1d.de/#profile=core&language=c&specification=gl&api=gl%3D3.3&api=gles2%3D3.0&extensions=GL_ARB_texture_storage&extensions=GL_EXT_texture_compression_s3tc&extensions=GL_EXT_texture_filter_anisotropic&extensions=GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax&extensions=GL_EXT_texture_sRGB&extensions=GL_OVR_multiview
*/
```

The generated files replaced, in version control, a version of glad.c and glad.h produced by exactly the same arguments except no `GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax`. The diff was as follows:
(This diff has been slightly hand edited for example I added some printfs.)

[addminmax.zip](https://github.com/Dav1dde/glad/files/1623941/addminmax.zip)
)

The following issues are seen:
1. By code inspection, the expected symbols for `GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax`  are not generated by GLAD. The docs for the extension are [here](https://www.khronos.org/registry/OpenGL/extensions/EXT/EXT_texture_filter_minmax.txt). I expect `TEXTURE_REDUCTION_MODE_EXT` and `WEIGHTED_AVERAGE_EXT` symbols. These are not in the diff, however in the diff for GLAD I do find symbols such as `glRasterSamplesEXT`, `RASTER_SAMPLES_EXT` and MULTISAMPLE_RASTERIZATION_ALLOWED_EXT being defined AND LOADED IN `load_GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax()`. In fact GLAD appears to be creating exactly those symbols used in `EXT_raster_multisample`, which I did not request (docs [here](https://developer.nvidia.com/sites/default/files/akamai/opengl/specs/GL_EXT_raster_multisample.txt)).
2. When the code is compiled, `GLAD_GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax` is a true value, yet the code:
```
#define GL_TEXTURE_REDUCTION_MODE_EXT 0x9366
#define GL_WEIGHTED_AVERAGE_EXT 0x9367
glTexParameteri(canvas->texture.id, GL_TEXTURE_REDUCTION_MODE_EXT, result);
```
Results in the OpenGL error 1280 Invalid Enum being generated.

Conclusion: GLAD appears to be reading the wrong extension out of the spec and mislabeling it as `GL_EXT_texture_filter_minmax`.

issue_title: Install instructions for Fish are wrong
issue_body:
The install instructions for Fish says to define

```fish
function fuck
    eval (thefuck (history | head -n1))
end
```

This won't work, because `history` will include the currently-executing command in its output, which means that this will always evaluate to `eval (thefuck fuck)`. It's also rather wasteful, as `history` dumps a bunch of history. There's also the curious property wherein if `thefuck` emits multiple lines, they'll end up being joined into a single line, as command substitution splits lines into arguments, and `eval` joins multiple arguments with spaces instead of newlines.

Both of these issues can be fixed by rewriting it as

```fish
function fuck
    thefuck $history[2] | source
end
```

My Will bot was working splendidly, then today I restarted him and he wouldn't join any rooms. You can still talk to him in private chat, but he doesn't show as being online in any of the channels he's supposed to join. In the room "users" list, his little hipchat circle is greyed out. It's still green next to his name in the private chat section, though.

On startup it says the he joined the rooms he was supposed to, but he's not actually there. I verified on the hipchat website that he had access to those rooms. Really not sure what's going on.

Some help in troubleshooting this would be greatly appreciated.

EDIT: Not sure if relevant, but sometimes when he starts it'll just show "Will has left the room" in the room. Other times not. Asking him `what are the rooms?` clearly shows all of the rooms he can access.

EDIT2: Turned on Debug Loglevel, and I saw where it was joining rooms it would look something like this:
`INFO Starting new HTTPS connection(): api.hipchat.com`
`DEBUG  "GET /v2/room/blahblah/?auth_token=blahblah;expand=xmpp_jid HTTP/1.1"`and each line for each room a had a 404 afterwards, some followed by a 97 and some followed by None. 
eg: `"GET blahblahblah HTTP/1.1" 404 97`

`api.hipchat.com/v2/room/ID/?auth_token=AUTHTOKEN` (which is the URL being posted in DEBUG output) throws a 404 and says it can't find the room

issue_title: ConstraintNumericalLeastSquares without bounds
issue_body:
If you fit using ``ConstraintNumbericalLeastSquares`` without specifying bounds:

```python
xdata, ydata = np.genfromtxt('fit_data.txt').T

x = Variable(name='x')
A = Parameter(name='A', value=200)
sig = Parameter(name='sig', value=50)
x0 = Parameter(name='x0', value=500)
# Gaussian distrubution
model = A*exp(-((x - x0)**2/(2 * sig**2)))

fit = ConstrainedNumericalLeastSquares(model, xdata, ydata)
fit_result = fit.execute()
print(fit_result)
print(model)
```

You get the following error message:

```
C:\Miniconda3\envs\py3_new\lib\site-packages\scipy\optimize\slsqp.py:341: RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in greater
  bnderr = bnds[:, 0] > bnds[:, 1]C:\Miniconda3\envs\py3_new\lib\site-packages\scipy\optimize\slsqp.py:341: RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in greater
  bnderr = bnds[:, 0] > bnds[:, 1]
```

This is because ``self.models.bounds`` is in this case ``[(None, None), (None, None), (None, None)]`` which does not evaluate to ``None`` when checked by [``scipy slsqp``](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/master/scipy/optimize/slsqp.py) (line 330). Its doesnt affect the fit in this case, but I can image it can cause problems.

issue_title: pdfdict's `__getitem__` may raise an error when requesting indirect objects
issue_body:
I have some code to extract images from a PDF, which works as follows (excuse me for the long code):

```python
# Many thanks to
# http://stackoverflow.com/questions/2693820/extract-images-from-pdf-without-resampling-in-python
# (converted from PyPDF2 to pdfrw)
from PIL import Image
from io import BytesIO

def write_xobject_to_image(xobject, out):
	if xobject['/Subtype'] == '/Image':
		size = (xobject['/Width'], xobject['/Height']) # Sometimes(!) raises an error
		data = xobject.stream
		if isinstance(data, str):
			data = data.encode('latin1') # encoding??

		if xobject['/ColorSpace'] == '/DeviceRGB':
			mode = "RGB"
		else:
			mode = "P"

		filetype = None
		# This is stupid.  Sometimes it's a string, sometimes an array...
		if isinstance(xobject['/Filter'], str):
			filters = [xobject['/Filter']]
		else:
			filters = xobject['/Filter']

		if '/FlateDecode' in filters:
			filetype = 'png'
			img = Image.frombytes(mode, size, data)
			img.save(out)
		elif '/DCTDecode' in filters:
			filetype = 'jpg'
			out.write(data)
		elif '/JPXDecode' in filters:
			filetype = 'jp2'
			out.write(data)
		# else: error?

		# Stop for first image on page
		return filetype

	return None


pdf_document = PdfReader(pdf_file)
for pageno in range(0, len(pdf_document.pages)):
	page = pdf_document.pages[pageno]

	images = find_objects(page, valid_subtypes=(PdfName.Image,))
	for img in images:
		with NamedTemporaryFile() as temp:
			write_xobject_to_image(img, temp)
```

Some PDFs will result in an error on the indicated line: `pdfrw.errors.PdfParseError: Dict key '/Height' is not a PdfName`

After some digging, it turns out that this is because `__getitem__()` in the `PdfDict` class accepts a string, which it passes to `get()`, which will convert it to  `PdfName`. Then if it turns out to be an indirect object, it will call `__setattr__` with the _original_ key, which is still a string, resulting in the aforementioned error.

Interestingly, if you change the code to add debugging info (like `print(xobject.keys())`), it will magically work (probably because indirect objects have already been resolved). I'm not sure how to fix this, nor how to even approach writing a test for this (I don't have much of a clue about how PDFs work), but this seems inconsistent: I'd expect the code to work, independent of whether the PDF has indirect objects or not.

I've now fixed this in my own code by removing all the square brackets, so it relies on `__getattr__` (which will always pass a `PdfName` to `get()`) instead of `__getitem__`. It now reads like this:

```python
def write_xobject_to_image(xobject, out):
	if xobject.Subtype == '/Image':
		size = (xobject.Width, xobject.Height)
		data = xobject.stream
		if isinstance(data, str):
			data = data.encode('latin1') # encoding??

		if xobject.ColorSpace == '/DeviceRGB':
			mode = "RGB"
		else:
			mode = "P"

		filetype = None
		# This is stupid.  Sometimes it's a string, sometimes an array...
		if isinstance(xobject.Filter, str):
			filters = [xobject.Filter]
		else:
			filters = xobject.Filter

		if '/FlateDecode' in filters:
			filetype = 'png'
			img = Image.frombytes(mode, size, data)
			img.save(out)
		elif '/DCTDecode' in filters:
			filetype = 'jpg'
			out.write(data)
		elif '/JPXDecode' in filters:
			filetype = 'jp2'
			out.write(data)
		# else: error?

		# Stop for first image on page
		return filetype

	return None
```

issue_title: Training object detector on 1000 images with upsample 3 is killed.
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
Hello. I want to train hog object detector on own dataset. 
http://dlib.net/train_object_detector.cpp.html 

Objects have one class. I have dataset with 12 000 images. First, I train object detector on 100 images. Then I try to train object detector on 1000 images with upsample 3 and I get error:

## Current Behavior
> Loading image dataset from metadata file final_dataset/dlib_dataset/training1000.xml
> Number of images loaded: 1001
> Killed

## Steps to Reproduce
-Compile dlib
-Compile script
`g++ train_object_detector.cpp -std=c++11 -O3 -I.. ../dlib/all/source.cpp -lpthread -lX11 -ljpeg -lpng -DDLIB_JPEG_SUPPORT -DDLIB_PNG_SUPPORT`
-Run script
`./a.out -tv final_dataset/dlib_dataset/training1000.xml -u3`

* **Version**: 19.8.99
* **Where did you get dlib**: git clone https://github.com/davisking/dlib
* **Platform**: Ubuntu 16.04 xenial 64 minimal
* **Compiler**: g++ 4.8.5 from anaconda

issue_title: Is there a way to tell dlib that boost is somewhere other than it expects?
issue_body:
I'm trying to put dlib on an AWS Lambda instance, which basically means I need dilb and boost in the same self-referencing folder structure.

e.g.
/anything

I haven't been able to find a way to make dlib work like this. It might help if compiling using setup.py could see the BOOST_LIBRARYDIR and BOOST_ROOT environment variables, which would presumably allow me to use relative paths.

issue_title: Travis CI build fails with OpenJDK 7 due to InvalidKeyException
issue_body:
Obviously, OpenJDK 7 is not able to access `plugins.gradle.org` due to missing elliptic curve cryptography support and fails with `java.security.InvalidKeyException: EC parameters error`.

For details see https://github.com/gradle/gradle/issues/2421.

Hi,
I added keyring as directory to my project, not via easy_install/pip, to make sure it is available on the target system. When I run it on OS X I get this:

```
w73mc-imac:Nagstamon hw5$ ./nagstamon.py

issue_title: Backend class prioritization
issue_body:
I'd like to do some architectural re-organization.

Instead of the existing implementation, which requires a backend class to be instantiated before it can be determined to be suitable, let's implement the 'supported' aspect as a classmethod, so classes can be determined to be suitable or not before instantiation.

Furthermore, instead of the supported attribute returning numbers -1, 0, and 1, let's supply instead a 'priority', which is a numeric value that can vary based on the environment. It should return None if it's not suitable and a positive number otherwise.

issue_title: Python Rules Swallow Error Messages
issue_body:
Hi,

First, thanks so much for this awesome package. I've been playing around with it for a while and am super impressed. 

One of the issues I'm having is that the rule functions in Python will swallow any errors they encounter rather than bubbling them up. Here's an example where the rule tries to call the function "errorOutHere" which is undefined and fails. Rather than printing any error to console or some notification it just fails silently. Is this intended behavior? Is there a log of these somewhere or perhaps a debug mode to see these issues?

```
from durable.lang import *

with ruleset('coach'):
    @when_all(m.completed != True)
    def trigger_reminders(c):
        print('An error message should follow this line')
        # this function is not defined and should crash
        errorOutHere()
        # this line is never reached
        print('This part is never reached and no error is thrown')

    @when_start
    def start(host):
        host.assert_fact('coach', {'id': 1, 'sid': 1, 'completed': False})

run_all()
```

issue_title: Investigate GIL_MINSIZE
issue_body:
# Problem Description

I think that the current `GIL_MINSIZE`, which is 2048, may be too small. For example, if you are reading a file and using the `st_blksize` from the `os.stat()` call you will very likely read 4096 bytes at a time:
```python
>>> import os
>>> os.stat('/etc/passwd').st_blksize
4096
```
If you are in a typical read/update loop, Python will switch threads during the reads anyway. So we will end up with a bunch of extra locking/unlocking (both for the lock on the object itself and the GIL) that does not provide any real improved threading behavior.

# KISS: Change 2048 to 8192
The simplest solution would be to change `GIL_MINSIZE` to 8192 and declare victory, since this should be larger than the typical `st_blksize` on most modern file systems. (This won't help with people too smart for their own good - like me - who will often use `st_blksize * 2` or `st_blksize * 4`. It also won't help with programs reading data from fast network interfaces who often get more than 8192 bytes at a time. But obviously you can't solve all the problems of the world...)

# Fancy-Pants solution: Make GIL_MINSIZE a variable
A more sophisticated solution would be to change `GIL_MINSIZE` to a variable, either global or per-instance. As part of this you would probably want to allow a user to request that `update()` never release the GIL; this makes sense for non-threaded code running on systems with thread support (or possibly on systems that use threading for convenience and are not very interested in concurrency).

I realize that exposing `GIL_MINSIZE` in a variable may make the module more complicated than desired, and may also result in many users pointlessly tweaking the value (there is a lot of superstition in the technical world). However I think it _can_ be done in a way that lets people who want specific values to get their desired behavior.

# Next Steps
I'm happy to implement either approach (either bumping the `GIL_MINSIZE` up or adding a variable) and issuing a pull request. I can also do some benchmarking and see what (if any) impact this would likely have.

issue_title: objects.merge docs don't match implementation
issue_body:
In particular, docs for merge say:

```
If the callback returns undefined merging will be handled by the method instead.
```

`undefined` in this context appears to be an artifact of a port from lodash... and looking at the implementation, the result of the callback is not considered.

The easy fix is to delete that sentence. Perhaps better would be to implement support for that functionality, perhaps by testing for `is NoValue`, and update the documentation to match that.

issue_title: Operate on the same files that pep8/pycodestyle would
issue_body:
With `pep8`/`pycodestyle`, it isn't necessary to specify a file target or `-r somedirectories`. It basically looks recursively for `*.py` automatically.

This is nice, because I can set my `exclude` option in one of the supported config files, and then

- I don't have to specify files/directories when invoking the command manually.
- I can also invoke it from places like `make` targets and the same `excludes` will apply. I don't have to maintain a list of files/directories in the command executed in the `Makefile` or other places.

flake8 works exactly the same way. Thus I can get *reports* of violations in a way that's nicely consistent between the two tools, but to *fix* them `autopep8` requires me to separately configure and maintain lists.

It'd be great if `autopep8`'s behavior was the same as `pycodestyle` and `flake8` in this regard.

issue_title: Jedi doesn't complete inside modules with a name equal to their containing package.
issue_body:
Say we have a project with the following structure:

```
$tree
.
├── a.py
└── b
    ├── __init__.py
    ├── b.py
    └── c.py
```

If we try to get completions from inside file b/b.py, jedi fails to do so.
For example, in the following test script we see in which situations we can get completions.

```
import sys
import jedi

sys.path.append("/tmp/test")

source = """
from a import A
from b.b import B
from b.c import C
A().
B().
C().
"""

fmt = "{:15} {:20} {:15}"
print(fmt.format('file', 'line to complete', 'len(jedi.Script(...).completions())'))
print()

for p in ('test.py', 'b/b.py', 'b/test.py'):
    for line_num in (5, 6, 7):
        line = source.splitlines()[line_num - 1]
        script = jedi.Script(source, line_num, len(line), '/tmp/test/' + p)
        print(fmt.format(p, line, len(script.completions())))
    print()
```

Which outputs:

```
file            line to complete     len(jedi.Script(...).completions())

test.py         A().                              22
test.py         B().                              22
test.py         C().                              22

b/b.py          A().                              22
b/b.py          B().                               0
b/b.py          C().                               0

b/test.py       A().                              22
b/test.py       B().                               0
b/test.py       C().                              22
```

As we can see, we can't get completions in module 'b/b.py' for other modules in package 'b'.
Additionaly, modules from package 'b' such as 'b/c.py' cant get completions for 'b/b.py'.

Python 3.5.2
Linux 4.7.2-1-ARCH GNU/Linux
jedi 0.9.0 (from emacs anaconda-mode v0.1.5)

issue_title: Support for Weakref in __slots__
issue_body:
Hello,
I see you added support for the __slots__ mechanism in the ast. Nice. However, this breaks cffi downstream, since they use weak references to Enum, etc objects (see their cparser.py file). In turn this breaks petlib which I maintain.
I think the solution is to include the "__weakref__" in the "__slots__" list of fields, as per the advice at:
https://docs.python.org/2/reference/datamodel.html#slots
Many thanks,
George

issue_title: [XEP-0084] Allow access to the original id (sha-1 of the image) to prevent issues with case sensitivity
Crochet's shutdown strategy is problematic
issue_body:
1. A thread named MainThread doesn't always exist (see #69, some comments #78). 
2. Shutting down when the main thread exits may be the wrong thing to do; perhaps the main thread starts a thread pool of workers and then exits.

Perhaps it would be better to just set all the crochet threads to be daemon threads.
Downsides:
1. No reactor shutdown events.
2. An occasional segfault? Though maybe modern Python is better than that.

issue_title: Integrating Bezier curves
issue_body:
In svg.path from PiP, there is a following commet:
```
# Apparently it's impossible to integrate a Cubic Bezier, so
# this is a geometric approximation instead.
```

Well, Bezier cubes are polynomials, and AFAIK polynomials are one of simplest functions to integrate (well, sin and cos may be simpler).

So unless I'm missing some crucial detail here, computing the length of a Bezier curve can be done analytically, with major speedup.

On a side note: it should be noted that Path.point() does not guarantee points being equally distant on curve when linearly changing argument is used. Wighting dispatching of Path.point to segment by segment weights helps, but Bezier curves internally don't behave that way.

issue_title: Match path to basePath
issue_body:
The OAS specification says that ["The path is appended to the `basePath` in order to construct the full URL."](http://swagger.io/specification/#pathsObject)

This (to me) implies that, to match a path defined in an OAS schema, a path would need to include the `basePath`. But in flex, [if a path doesn't include the `basePath`, it's matched to the schema paths anyway.](https://github.com/pipermerriam/flex/blob/8419783f293632258b5ce15c0737e620f9271bd3/flex/paths.py#L134)

The impact of this is, if an app has two endpoints: `/cat` and `/v1/cat`, and an OAS schema with a `basePath` of `'v1'`, responses from `/cat` could be validated against the schema for `/v1/cat`. Instead, I'd expect attempting to validate a response from `/cat` to raise a `LookUpError` with no matching paths found.

I'd be happy to fix this if you agree that it's a bug.

Here's my kitten:
https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/content_link/7W3bWo0D1uOYXG5MlUtSOKBIIKsPoPZgBjiTZtZla3UxkDY8hqALMqNRqMsFDPqK/file

issue_title: pyodbc.connect abort on macOS with ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server
issue_body:
Perhaps this issue may be bug on msodbcsql17 (not on pyodbc).

### Maybe related issues

- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50361149/sql-server-pyodbc-driver-issue-libcabi-dylib-terminating-with-uncaught-excep
- https://github.com/michiya/django-pyodbc-azure/issues/164

### Environment

To diagnose, we usually need to know the following, including version numbers.  On Windows, be
sure to specify 32-bit Python or 64-bit:

- Python: 3.6
- pyodbc: latest master (3d14d4) on GitHub, 4.0.23, 4.0.22
- OS: macOS (Darwin MBP-14-003 17.5.0 Darwin Kernel Version 17.5.0: Fri Apr 13 19:32:32 PDT 2018; root:xnu-4570.51.2~1/RELEASE_X86_64 x86_64)
- DB: SQL Server 2017
- driver: [ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server -- msodbcsql17](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-US/sql/connect/odbc/linux-mac/installing-the-microsoft-odbc-driver-for-sql-server?view=sql-server-2017)

### Issue

```python
import pyodbc
pyodbc.connect(driver='{ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server}', ...)
```

### expected behavior

- Successing without error. Or occuring usual database error.

### observed behavior

- Aborting with suddenly dies on C++ error. It cannot catching exception in python.

```
libc++abi.dylib: terminating with uncaught exception of type std::runtime_error: collate_byname<char>::collate_byname failed to construct for C/en_US.UTF-8/C/C/C/C
Abort trap: 6
```

And this error was not happened with ODBC Driver 13 for SQL Server.

### my mitigation

When resetting locale before connection, this error will not happen.

```python
import pyodbc
import locale
locale.resetlocale()
pyodbc.connect(driver='{ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server}', ...)
```

Or running python with environment variable `LC_CTYPE=C`, this error is suppressed.
But forcing locales like this mitigation breaks some backward compatibility.

issue_title: Inverse function of flatten
issue_body:
Given the reducer is 1-to-1, the flatten function should be 1-to-1.
Therefore it is possible to provide a function to invert the flattened dictionary.

issue_title: Bug: semicolon in rule message
issue_body:
If a semicolon (`;`) is present (escaped, obviously) in the rule `msg` option the parser gets confused.

Example:
```
$ my_ryle = '''alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"example of semicolon\; usage here"; classtype:trojan-activity; sid:9999; rev:1;)'''
$ parsed_rule = rule.parse(my_rule)
$ print parsed_rule.msg
'"example of semicolon\\'
$ print parsed_rule.options
[{'name': 'msg', 'value': '"example of semicolon\\'}, {'name': 'usage here"', 'value': None}, {'name': 'classtype', 'value': 'trojan-activity'}, {'name': 'sid', 'value': '9999'}, {'name': 'rev', 'value': '1'}]
```

Some rules (ETPRO) really use semicolon in the msg option.

issue_title: Event of remove_job in listener
issue_body:
Hi,
I'm trying to implement a functionality that when a job reaches its end time and remove by the scheduler.
Suppose this script:
```
from time import sleep
from pprint import pprint
from apscheduler.schedulers.background import BackgroundScheduler
from apscheduler.jobstores.sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemyJobStore
from apscheduler.triggers.date import DateTrigger
from apscheduler.triggers.interval import IntervalTrigger
from apscheduler.events import EVENT_JOB_EXECUTED, EVENT_JOB_ERROR, EVENT_JOB_REMOVED


def job_executed(event):
    pprint(vars(event))
    print("hello from job_executed")


def func():
    return "hello from func"


if __name__ == '__main__':
    jobstores = {
        'default': SQLAlchemyJobStore(url='sqlite:///test.db')
    }
    sched = BackgroundScheduler(jobstores=jobstores)
    sched.add_listener(job_executed, EVENT_JOB_EXECUTED | EVENT_JOB_ERROR | EVENT_JOB_REMOVED)

    trig = IntervalTrigger(seconds=0.5)
    sched.add_job(func, trigger=trig,
                  id='func', replace_existing=True,
                  max_instances=1)
    sched.start()
    sleep(10)
    sched.remove_job('func')
    print("Done!!!")
    while True:
        pass

```

> hello from job_executed
> {'alias': None,
>  'code': 4096,
>  'exception': None,
>  'job_id': 'func',
>  'jobstore': 'default',
>  'retval': 'hello from func',
>  'scheduled_run_time': datetime.datetime(2018, 8, 5, 10, 32, 0, 740587, tzinfo=<DstTzInfo 'Asia/Tehran' +0430+4:30:00 DST>),
>  'traceback': None}
> hello from job_executed
> {'alias': None, 'code': 1024, 'job_id': 'func', 'jobstore': 'default'}
> hello from job_executed
> Done!!!

Please consider that both `EVENT_JOB_EXECUTED` and `EVENT_JOB_REMOVED` must be on a same callback function. The `job_executed` function needs to know when the job gets removed by the scheduler. How can we do this? I see only `code` that is `1024` for `job_remove`. Is it true or there is another way that already implemented?
Thanks.

issue_title: Module-level functions or instance methods for helpers
issue_body:
There is a need to have a number of helper functions, such as asdict(), astuple(), replace(), etc. This issue is not about which specific functions we need (that will come later), but rather how to make these functions available.

namedtuple handles this by having member functions that begin with an underscore, such as ._make(), ._asdict(), ._replace(), ._fields(), etc.

attrs handles this by using module-level functions that take an instance as a parameter, such as .attrs.fields(), attrs.has(), attrs.asdict(), etc.

I'm leaning towards the attrs interface of using module-level functions. I don't like the underscores required by namedtuple's approach, and even then you could have name collisions.

issue_title: support for wifi370 led strip controllers
issue_body:
I have an (older?) wifi led strip controller, described as wifi370 on the box. 
https://nl.aliexpress.com/item/Original-Wifi-RGB-LED-controller-for-Iphone-Ipad-Android-mobile-phone-2-3Version-IOS-system-DC7/1032823060.html

this device is supported by the magic home app, and it seems to be a 'propper'  flux_led device, but it has some significant differences. 

this library recognizes it while scanning, but this device uses a different set of commands and does not use a checksum to send data.
eg, turn on is  CC 23 33 rather than 71 23 0F
status is EF 01 77 rather than 81 8A 8B

this device also returns only 11 bytes of status rather than the 14 this library expects.

I managed to do a python conversion based on https://github.com/sidoh/ledenet_api

it can be found here: https://github.com/marcwagner/alarmclock/blob/master/ledenet_api.py

I can probably integrate my code into this project so that it can also work with homeassistant. However, due to some of the differences I am worried about breaking compatibility. 

so, two questions: should I integrate my code and is there an easy way to recognize the different flavors of protocol?

issue_title: Raise error when trying to create parameter that accepts an array of files
issue_body:
Apparently swagger 2.0 can't handle an array of files (see [this issue](https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/823) on swagger's api) as a parameter, maybe flask-restplus should raise an error if the user tries to do this.

I ran into this while trying to add an argument with something like this
```python
parser.add_argument('image', type=load_image, location='files',
                    required=True, action='append')
```

The swagger specification that is generated for that parameter looks like
```json
{
    "collectionFormat": "multi", 
    "description": "", 
    "in": "formData", 
    "items": {
        "type": "file"
    }, 
    "name": "image", 
    "required": true, 
    "type": "array"
}, 
```

issue_title: Comparison of Numerical, Date, Boolean types aren't working
issue_body:
Because it casts the response data to text in default, the comparisons for numerical, date, and boolean types are failing.
Ex:
```
self.assertEqual(value, text_type(data[attr]))
E   AssertionError: 10032 != '10032'

self.assertEqual(value, text_type(data[attr]))
E   AssertionError: '2017-03-13 12:21:13.796885+00:00' != '2017-03-13T12:21:13+0000'

self.assertEqual(value, text_type(data[attr]))
E   AssertionError: False != 'False'
```

I think we should determine the type of fields instead of simply casting into text.

issue_title: Error loading template: 'crispy_forms_tags' is not a registered tag library
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm sorry for the issue title. I couldn't think of a better title.

I get the following error message when running `./manage.py compilescss`:

```
Invalid template [...]/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/rest_framework/
templates/rest_framework/filters/django_filter_crispyforms.html: 
'crispy_forms_tags' is not a registered tag library. Must be one of:
admin_list
admin_modify
admin_static
admin_urls
bootstrap3
cache
compress
future
i18n
l10n
log
rest_framework
sass_tags
static
staticfiles
tz
```

The application uses Django REST framework, but not django-crispy-forms (it's an optional dependency). I figured out that django-sass-processor loads all templates to look for `sass_src` tags ([sass_processor/management/commands/compilescss.py#L85][1]). This means mean the django-crispy-forms templates included with Django REST framework also get loaded even if we're not using them.

After thinking for a while I thought a template blacklist would be a good solution. What do you think about it?

Thanks,
Frank

[1]: https://github.com/jrief/django-sass-processor/blob/master/sass_processor/management/commands/compilescss.py#L85

issue_title: Documentation says 'report_path' instead of 'path'
issue_body: In nose2.cfg or unittest.cfg's `[html-report]` section, the key name to specify report path should be `path`. It is incorrectly documented as `report_path`. Using `report_path` has no effect, whereas `path` works as expected.

begin_date = models.DateTimeField(db_column='period_begin_date')
```

After migrating, it appears in the database as:

```
period_begin_date(datetime2(7), not null)
```

So far, so good. When I insert fields, they're getting inserted correctly into SQL Server. However, when I get an object from the Python shell:

```
----> 1 print(bt.begin_period.month)
AttributeError: 'Period' object has no attribute 'month'
```

Any ideas why? Is it the FreeTDS version conflicting on types?

issue_body:
Trying to execute:

```
# Technicallity: we want to use the regexp based tokenizer
# that is used by CountVectorizer  and only use the lemmatization
# from SpaCy. To this end, we replace en_nlp.tokenizer (the SpaCy tokenizer)
# with the regexp based tokenization
import re
# regexp used in CountVectorizer:
regexp = re.compile('(?u)\\b\\w\\w+\\b')

# load spacy language model and save old tokenizer
en_nlp = spacy.load('en')
old_tokenizer = en_nlp.tokenizer
# replace the tokenizer with the preceding regexp
en_nlp.tokenizer = lambda string: old_tokenizer.tokens_from_list(
    regexp.findall(string))

# create a custom tokenizer using the SpaCy document processing pipeline
# (now using our own tokenizer)
def custom_tokenizer(document):
    doc_spacy = en_nlp(document, entity=False, parse=False)
    return [token.lemma_ for token in doc_spacy]

# define a count vectorizer with the custom tokenizer

issue_title: models with ID columns not named ID cause exception
issue_body:
With a model that has an ID column not named ID, and the following code:

class TestView(PandasView):
    queryset = WeirdIDModel.objects.all()
    serializer_class = WeirdIDModelSerializer
   
when trying to query the class, you get an error KeyError, "id".

Reading that stack trace and the source I see that this can be fixed in two obvious ways:

set pandas_index on the meta of the serializer
override get_index_fields on the serializer

setting the index seems to be needless duplication (I've already written once what the index of this model is)
an override to get_index_fields I think would just create behaviour that should be default:
        WeirdIDModelSerializer._meta.pk.name gives the ID, for my version of Django at least

so something like 
try:
    id_name = (self.insance?)._meta.pk.name
except:
   id_name = 'id'
default_fields = [id_name]

Thinking about it more, and reading the source, it seems odd to even set an index, especially without preserving the original column - I think that DRF classes that we're mimicking would by default pass the index through to the client, and since I'm trying to drop-in replace some existing code which might rely on that, I'll have to replicate that manually.

issue_title: Compacting with @vocab does not include already compacted terms
Help text for form fields
issue_body:
Bootstrap 4 uses a combination of `.form-text` and `.text-muted` to replace the old `.help-block` class for specifying help text on form elements. PR #49 makes this change in the django-bootstrap4 project code, but it keeps the help text in a `<div>` element, whereas the Bootstrap docs use a `<small>` instead (https://getbootstrap.com/docs/4.0/components/forms/#help-text), which renders smaller text.

The lack of a semantic classname (`.help-block` was great) makes it harder to isolate and target help text but it's still possible. `.form-group .form-text.text-muted {font-size: 80%}` works for me, but I wonder if it would be better to change the `field_help_text_and_errors.html` [template](https://github.com/zostera/django-bootstrap4/blob/master/bootstrap4/templates/bootstrap4/field_help_text_and_errors.html) to render a `<small>` instead of a `<div>`, so that a `{% bootstrap_field %}` template tag for a field with help text will match what is shown in the Bootstrap examples.

issue_title: Extract data from json array
excluding products already available offline from SentinelAPI.download_all
issue_body:
Good day,
I have been using sentinelsat for quite a while and had to script my way around one limitation:
When using the` SentinelAPI.download_all` with the result of the `query` method (the uuids) as input there is no convenient way to skip specific products without manually filtering this `products` input list. I would love to see this feature in `sentinelsat ` and have implemented it in my local repo. Mabye its a helpful addition for other users as well? 
So here is what I propose:
A new argument to `SentinelAPI.download_all` called `skip_products` that takes as input a list of uuids and/or root folders of products to skip and filters them out from the input argument `products`.


Just to elaborate on why I deem this feature important:
I am working on windows using SARscape to regularly process relatively large stacks of Sentinel-1 data with constant region, sensor, accquisition mode etc (excluding a temporal constraint). Within this workflow I need to unzip and rename the obtained products. I regularly want to download recently published products using the same query. The problem is: Even if I choose the same output directory, all the products that where already downloaded would be downloaded again, since only products with exactly the same filename (local vs. online) are skipped.
Thanks a lot by the way for the great library!

issue_title: Better error message for missing namespace resource
Can connect to Skyscanner API
issue_body:
Hi, I've asked the same question in StackOverflow and to the Skyscanner Support team, but haven't received yet an answer to my question. On both sides people are suggesting me to use the `skyscanner` library, which is based on the `requests` library. I don't have anything against it, but the purpose of my question is to learn how to connect to an API, because I want to start using them more often. I read in Dive Into Python 3 good things about `httplib2`, thus I want to start using it.

I'm trying to establish a connection via an http request to the Skyscanner API. To do so, I wrote the following code:

```
import sys
import httplib2
from urllib.parse import urlencode


class Connection(object):
    """Connect to Skyscanner Business API and retrieve quotes"""

    API_HOST = "http://partners.api.skyscanner.net"
    PRICING_SESSION_URL = "{api_host}/apiservices/pricing/v1.0".format(
        api_host=API_HOST)
    SESSION_HEADERS = {"Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencode",
                       "Accept": "application/json"}

    def __init__(self, api_key="prtl6749387986743898559646983194", body=None):
        """
        :param api_key: the API key, the default is an API key provided for
        testing purposes in the Skyscanner API documentation
        :param body: the details of the flight we are interested on
        """

        if not api_key:
            raise ValueError("The API key must be provided")

        self.api_key = api_key
        self.body = body
        self.create_session(api_key=self.api_key, body=self.body)

    def get_result(self):
        pass

    def make_request(self, service_url, method="GET", headers=None, body=None):
        """Perform either a POST or GET request

        :param service_url: URL to request
        :param method: request method, default is GET
        :param headers: request headers
        :param data: the body of the request
        """

        if "apikey" not in service_url.lower():
            body.update({
                "apikey": self.api_key
            })

        h = httplib2.Http(".cache")
        response, content = h.request(service_url,
                                      method=method,
                                      body=urlencode(body),
                                      headers=headers)
        print(str(response))

    def create_session(self, api_key, body):
        """Create the Live Pricing Service session"""

        return self.make_request(self.PRICING_SESSION_URL,
                                 method="POST",
                                 headers=self.SESSION_HEADERS,
                                 body=body)

def main():
    body = {
        "country": "UK",
        "currency": "GBP",
        "locale": "en-GB",
        "originplace": "LOND-sky",
        "destinationplace": "NYCA-sky",
        "outbounddate": "2016-05-01",
        "inbounddate": "2016-05-10",
        "adults": 1
    }

    Connection(body=body)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

What the code above does is to send a POST request to their API in order to create a Live Pricing session. To retrieve the data I should then send a GET request with the URL to poll the booking details. This URL is provided in the Location header of the POST response.

The API key is publicly available on their documentation, as it's a generic one they suggest to use for experimenting with their API. I'm sure the key is working as I've replicated the POST request using the same details on the code above using Postman, a Google Chrome extension to test APIs sending HTTP requests. Using Postman I can successfully establish a connection (Status: 201 Created).

However, if I run the code above I get the following response:

```
{'content-length': '0', 'date': 'Sun, 24 Apr 2016 08:44:23 GMT', 'status': '415', 'cache-control': 'private'}
```

The documentation doesn't explicitly say what the Status 415 represents. Anyway, do you see anything wrong with how I'm sending the request?

Apologies for the quality of my code. I'm a beginner.

issue_title: Use "release candidate" builds
issue_body:
For mostly historical reasons, the Deis release process begins with an intended final build (v1.11.0, let's say) rather than a release candidate build (v1.11.0-rc1 perhaps). This build may be updated inline once or more during final testing as showstopper bugs are found.

Instead, the Deis project could produce more traditional release candidate builds and make them generally available, which would allow the community to help with testing as well. A smoke test on multiple providers must pass cleanly before the release candidate is publicized for testing.

Caveats here are:
- Need to ensure that upgrade testing will work smoothly with v1.11.0-rc1 - type tags
- The project tags are clean and useful now--can we avoid pushing a `git` tag of "v1.11.0-rc1", or can we delete it afterward safely?
- `deisctl` is intimately tied to git tags and needs a custom -rc1 release
- versions are splattered throughout the codebase so each release candidate will require `bumpver` work
- ideally we just re-tag Docker components, not rebuild, since `docker build` doesn't allow us precise control over the bits

The [`notary`](https://github.com/docker/notary) tool from Docker provides one way to secure this workflow by adding an additional piped command that ensures content verification.

issue_title: Allow 'TimeDependent' functions for Batching_distributions
issue_body:
Was there a specific reason not to accept them?

I already tried with a quick-and-dirty change to the existing code to accept them and apparently it works.

I have a use case where i must simulate a take-away pizzeria.
When a new ordering comes in, I must check if it's acceptable or not.
Each ordering has a quantity of pizzas and the requested time of pickup.
Let's say the oven can accomodate 4 pizzas at a time, so I have 4 servers.
A pizza takes 5 minutes to cook, so it must enter the system exactly 5 minutes before the requested pickup time.
In this case i do not have a probabilistic distribution of arrivals, because I know exactly when each pizza must enter the oven.
But each ordering may request more than one pizza so, in addition to a 'TimeDependent' function for next time of arrival, I need a 'TimeDependent' function for number of individuals entering the system each time.
I could then run a simulation using the already accepted orderings AND the new one and see if it gets out of the system in time for pickup.
If it does not, I'll refuse it.
I'm new to queue theory, so I'm not sure if this is the best way to model such a use case.
If it is, then 'TimeDependent' functions should be allowed for Batching_distributions.

Thanks a lot!!!

issue_title: Expose `pluggy._CallOutcome` as part of the public API
issue_body:
Currently [`pluggy._CallOutcome`](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/master/pluggy.py#L200) is implied to be part of the internal api by the leading `_`.

In practice it is used by [`hookwrappers`](https://pluggy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#wrappers).

An example:
```python
@pytest.hookimpl(tryfirst=True, hookwrapper=True)
def pytest_runtest_makereport(item, call):
    outcome = yield
    rep = outcome.get_result() 
    ...
```
The `outcome` is accessed directly by the user.
Therefore I propose we expose it correct in `plugg.__all__` and expose it in the [api reference](https://pluggy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api_reference.html).

issue_title: Use https://github.com/cgoldberg/xvfbwrapper for xvfb management
Make it possible to change the query file without having to rebuild the entire database
issue_body:
Currently we use the md5 of the query file as the lookup for checkpoints but any modifications to the file change the md5 and cause a rebuild.

Particularly for large databases this may not be desired and users need more control over when a rebuild occurs.

In order to maintain the current workflow but still enable the new workflow a new command line argument should be added to provide a 'checkpoint key'. This key will then be used to maintain the checkpoint information and a change to the key will result in a full rebuild.

A change to the following arguments should also result in a rebuild (regardless of the checkpoint key):
* project space (--project arg)
* HQ instance being queried (--commcare-hq arg)

issue_body:
I just realized that the ```AirborneRadarDisplay.plot_sweep_grid``` was inadvertently broken when a combination of things occurred. 
First, AirborneRadarDisplay was made a subclass of RadarDisplay, thus some methods are superseded. 

Second, LazyLoad _get_x_y_z calculates Cartesian space based upon ```pyart.core.transforms.antenna_vectors_to_cartesian```. For a tail radar such as the NOAA P-3 (for which this class was created), the calculation should actually use ```pyart.core.transforms.antenna_to_cartesian_track_relative``` which uses a coordinate transformation for aircraft using rotation.

How do you think this should be fixed? A conditional statement could be placed somewhere along the path (e.g. in ```pyart.core.radar.get_gate_x_y_z``` that would check the platform and assign the correct lazy load gate calculation.

Instead of

```python
 def __init__(self,
              service_path=SERVICE_ADDRESS,
              port=DEFAULT_SERVICE_PORT,
              channel=None,
              ssl_creds=None,

do

```python
  _GAX_VERSION  = pkg_resource.get_distribution('google.gax').version) # a class constant

 def __init__(self,
              service_path=SERVICE_ADDRESS,
              port=DEFAULT_SERVICE_PORT,
              channel=None,
              ssl_creds=None,
              scopes=None,
              retrying_override=None,

issue_title: letsencrypt on shared hosting
issue_body:
I had problems with the manual method at All-inkl.com

At least I made my way easy and made with:
```mkdir -p .well-known/acme-challenge/```
a directory in the webroot.

Startet letsencrypt with:
```./letsencrypt-auto auth -d DOMAINNAME --authenticator manual```
Then I wrote a php-file with name well-known/acme-challenge/$CRYPTICNAME.php and the content:
```<?php
$data = '{"header": {"alg": "RS256", "jwk": {"e": "AQAB", "kty": "RSA", "n": "blablabla"}}, "payload": "blabla", "signature": "blabla"}';
header('Content-Type: application/jose+json');
echo $data;
?> 
```
It worked.

IMO there should be an automatism to generate the content of this php-files. 
It will be  easier to use letsencrypt for customers of webhosting plattforms

issue_title: Fail to create parser using a big grammar (memory increase infinitely)
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm trying to parse a text in python 3.5, using the 0.5.6 release of lark.
I have a very long grammar in this format :

```
start: title  field+

field: rule1 -> alias1
	| rule2 -> alias2
	[…]
	| rule386 -> alias386

//AUXILIARY TERMS
title: ...
term1: ...
[...]
term90:

//RULES GROUP 1
rule1: ...
[...]
rule276: ...

//RULES GROUP2
rule277: ...
[...]
rule386: ...

//TERMINALS
[...]
```

Here are some examples of the syntax of rules and terms : 
 ```
//AUXILIARY TERMS
adexpmsg: CHARACTER*
aidequipment: (("N"|"S") [equipmentcode])|equipmentcode
aircraftid: ALPHANUM~2..7

//PRIMARY FIELDS
aatot: _HYPHEN _sep "AATOT" _sep timehhmm
ad: _HYPHEN _sep "AD" _sep adid [_sep (fl|flblock)] [_sep eto] [_sep to] [_sep cto] [_sep sto] [_sep ptstay] [_sep ptrfl] [_sep ptrulchg] [_sep (ptspeed|ptmach)]
ada: _HYPHEN _sep "ADA" _sep date

//SUBFIELDS
addrinfo: _HYPHEN _sep "ADDRINFO" _sep networktype _sep fac
adid: _HYPHEN _sep "ADID" _sep (icaoaerodrome | "ZZZZ")
adname: _HYPHEN _sep "ADNAME" _sep (LIM_CHAR)~1..50

//TERMINALS
_sep: SEP*
ALPHA: /[A-Z]{1}/
DIGIT: /[0-9]{1}/
ALPHANUM: ALPHA|DIGIT
SPACE: " "
_HYPHEN: "-"
FEF: "\n"|"\r"
SEP: (SPACE|FEF)
SPECIAL: SPACE
	|"("
	|")"
	|"?"
	|":"
	|"."
	|","
	|"'"
	|"="
	|"+"
	|"/"
CHARACTER: ALPHA|DIGIT|SPECIAL|FEF|_HYPHEN
LIM_CHAR: ALPHA|DIGIT|SPECIAL|FEF
START_OF_FIELD: _HYPHEN
%import common.WS
```

The text format I'm trying to parse is the ADEXP format, which is a succession of fields, all of them beginning by a "-", followed by a name of field, and one or more values, which can also be a new field.
The first field is "-TITLE".
Here is an example of an ADEXP message :

```
-TITLE BFD -REFDATA -SENDER -FAC BORD -RECVR -FAC A -SEQNUM 001 -ARCID RYR743D -SSRCODE A1122 -NBARC 1 
-ARCTYP B738 -ADEP EDDH -ROUTE N0441F370 OLRAK DEGOL LAPRO PPG ALBER  -BEGIN RTEPTS -PT -PTID OLRAK -PT 
-PTID DEGOL -PT -PTID LAPRO -PT -PTID PPG -PT -PTID ALBER -END RTEPTS -ADES LEBL -BEGIN EQCST -EQPT Y/EQ 
-EQPT W/EQ -EQPT R/EQ -END EQCST -RFL F370 -SPEED N0441 -EOBT 2359 -WKTRC M
```

This format allow separators between almost every fields, but some fields have to be directly one after the other, without any separator, so i had to explicit all separators in all rules.
You can find in details the complete syntax of all fields [here](https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=2&ved=0ahUKEwjP_Ijkw4DcAhUCesAKHeUhBSUQFgg0MAE&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.eurocontrol.int%2Fdownload%2Fpublication%2Fnode-field_download-4907-0&usg=AOvVaw0xAxaodfoVbtJ10MvjN0E7).

I tested some rules individually and I'm able to parse a text with this rules.

But when I try to parse the entire grammar above (~900 lines), lark can't create the parser:

When I execute the code :

```
[...]
print("Creating parser")
parser = Lark(grammar, parser='earley')
print("Parser created")
tree_res = parser.parse(text)
print("Text parsed")
```

The program displays "Creating parser", and processes to create it. The RAM is increasing progressively, until it reaches 12-13GO of RAM and get killed by the system (after 15-20 minutes).

I also tried to use different parser with different lexers, but it doesn't change anything.
I obtained the same result when I used a different python interpreter (Pypy).

I would like to know if you have any idea why it's taking this time to finally get killed ? Is it my grammar which is too big ? Or maybe too ambiguous ?
Tell me if you need any more details about the grammar or anything else.

Thank you in advance for your answer.

issue_title: Parsing ISO date is broken when dayfirst=True (since 2.5.2 release)
issue_body:
Before 2.5.2 release, parse with dayfirst=True, parsed dates like '2015-02-06' as expected 2 – month and 06 – day. This is right, because `-` delimiter is only possible in ISO 8601. According to this list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Date_format_by_country, format yyyy-dd-mm is not used anywhere.

Seems, pull request https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/pull/229 broken this behavior.

Can we please fix it, because I need to parse '2015-02-06' as 6 February, but if date was given as 06.02.15 consider first number is a day.

issue_title: Add python_requires to setup.py
issue_labels: [{'id': 193509772, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/build', 'name': 'build', 'color': 'bfdadc', 'default': False}, {'id': 149492527, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/enhancement', 'name': 'enhancement', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': True}, {'id': 770220100, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'b43dff', 'default': True}, {'id': 149492528, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/help%20wanted', 'name': 'help wanted', 'color': '159818', 'default': True}, {'id': 667039516, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/low-difficulty', 'name': 'low-difficulty', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Right now we're just using the trove classifiers, so I think people may end up doing some complicated pinning, which is less than ideal (see #536 and #526). What we need is the `Requires-Python:` metadata in pip. I think this is added through `setup.py`. Here are some examples of people using it:

- [pytest](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/blob/master/setup.py#L86)
- [ipython](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/blob/master/setup.py#L235)

@carreau gave [this talk at PyBay](https://youtu.be/3i6n1RwqQCo?t=660) (link goes to the relevant time - 11 minutes in) where he discusses the `python_requires` option ([slides are here](https://speakerdeck.com/pybay/2017-building-bridges-stopping-python-2-without-damages)), probably best to listen to what he's talking about and avoid or document the pitfalls here.

issue_title: jira.group returns dict instead of list
Update API of PyObj
issue_body:
* Move into "python/obj.h|cc" file
* Rename into `py::_obj`
* Make 2 derived classes: `py::bobj` and `py::oobj` ("borrowed" and "owned" reference). If possible, these classes should not be virtual.
* Other classes (such as `py::string`, `py::long`, `py::list`, etc) should derive from `py::?obj`.

issue_title: Checkpoints will not reload from a run that was Ctrl-C'ed
issue_body:
If I use ctrl-c to kill a run, the checkpoint file will not load into a subsequent run:

```
2018-04-25 10:31:39 parsl.dataflow.dflow:692 [ERROR]  Failed to load Checkpoint: /home/benc/parsl/desc-skeleton/runinfo/001/checkpoint/tasks.pkl
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/benc/parsl/src/parsl/parsl/dataflow/dflow.py", line 672, in _load_checkpoints
    data = pickle.load(f)
TypeError: __init__() missing 2 required positional arguments: 'reason' and 'exitcode'

Z3 returns unknown for [test.smt2.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/1364246/test.smt2.txt) (at least on the current master, 7e4f5322).

However, after removing the extra `(check-sat)`s on lines 20 and 21, Z3 returns satisfiable (for `(check-sat)` at the end of the file).

On 1d12a9c86de, removing only one of the two sequential `(check-sat)`s was sufficient to make the query satisfiable. This seems even more surprising to me.

Apologies for the noise if issues like this are well-known; I just hadn't encountered this behavior before.

issue_title: Semantic change for TIMEOUT option between 4.4.1 and 4.5?
issue_body:
I'm currently trying out [Boogie](https://github.com/boogie-org/boogie) with Z3 4.5 and Boogie currently isn't working because Z3 is behaving differently when it sees

```
(set-option :TIMEOUT 0)
```

Previously Z3 would treat this as there being no timeout. Now Z3 treats this as being no time being allowed for solving which is pretty useless. So now Boogie makes no progress because all solver queries return `unknown` with the reason `canceled`.

I strongly suspect (but haven't checked) that this change is related to this change

https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/commit/a6e1c70eab988de59560c34a4222c99ce80e2feb

which was made in response to an issue I reported a while back ( https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/issues/445 ).

I can work around this in Boogie but this change might need rethinking. Why would anyone ever want to give Z3 no time to solver a query?

[Backup] Issues with `backup policy set`
issue_body:
I was very confused as to how to create a backup policy when I tried to enable backup on my VM. I tried to use the `get-default-for-vm` command in conjunction with `backup policy set` but I got errors. It turns out this was due to me not quoting the policy document. However, some suggestions:

- [ ] Accept `--name\-n` for this command. While this name is contained within the JSON document, it would be a much more intuitive experience to allow the user to specify via command line. Essentially when you parse the JSON object, if the user has specified name, it would patch the object with the new name. It would allow you to pipe the output of the "get-default.." command directly into the policy set command without an intervening step of having to use third-party tools to modify it.
- [ ] Update the help to clarify that the command is used to "Create a new backup policy or replace an existing one."  Since it is basically a PUT option, say it "updates" the policy is incorrect because in CLI 2.0, "update" specifically implies PATCH-like behavior.

---

issue_title: `az` help text
issue_body:
When there are mistakes in uses of the `az` cli, the output often comes out in a way that is not too helpful.  As a random example, see the below.  It looks like the output is made by printing the basic command+subs and then the list of arguments with some automatic line wrapping.  The thing is that the commands tend to be very nested and therefore very long, so there's little room for the argument list.  In particular, note that in my example the destination and source mandatory arguments are wrapped in a very confusing way.

It would be much better if the arguments were
* specified at a fixed indentation from the left side (or maybe following some "arguments:" header
* listed with only one argument per line
* listed in a better & consistent order, eg, mandatory arguments first, optional ones next, and finally generic ones (`-h` / `--output` / etc)

(As a side note, this style of command-line is very fitting for someone who is coming from a powershell background, but the overall design is weird in a linux world: like the lack of non-flagged ("positional") arguments, and the flags that have multiple values as in the `--metadata` below.)

    $ az storage blob upload-batch --destination "https://...windows.net/.../foo/" --source "..."
    usage: az storage blob upload-batch [-h] [--output {json,tsv,table,jsonc}]
                                        [--verbose] [--debug] [--query JMESPATH]

issue_title: [DNS] DNS record management commands should support if-match and if-none-match
issue_body:
The commands under 'az network dns record ...' are used to add/remove records from DNS record sets. They can also create a new record set if one does not already exist.

These operations need to support optimistic concurrency to protect against concurrent changes.

To address this, the '--if-match <value>' parameter should be supported on both the 'add' and 'remove' commands (and 'update-soa').

When the record set doesn't already exist and thus a new record set is being created, the command should *always* include '--if-none-match *' to protect against concurrent operations. This this should always be applied in this case, there is no need to expose it in the command parameters.

issue_title: Allow specifying coverage configuration in `python_test` targets.
issue_body:
Ideally, it would be possible to specify a coverage configuration in the target definition of a `python_test` target.  Perhaps something like this:

```python
python_tests(name='test',
  dependencies=[
    'src/python/my_package:my_package',
  ],
  sources=globs('*.py'),
  coverage=True
  coverage_config='.coveragerc'
)
```

Some discussion of this approach would be helpful. There is the question of whether the specification for the coverage config file should be a project root-relative path, or a simple filename which must correspond to a file in the root of the test sources.  

It might even be worth having the default behavior be to seek a `.coveragerc` file in the test source root folder, and have this be something that could be overridden by passing a command line option, or specifying a config file in the target definition.  In this scenario, without specifying a file simply invoking coverage would use a `.coveragerc` file if present.  That might be a bit too magical though.

issue_title: Hide self from step parameters by default
issue_body:
#### I'm submitting a ... 
  - [ ] bug report
  - [x] feature request
  - [ ] support request => Please do not submit support request here, see note at the top of this template.

#### What is the current behavior?

```python
class SomeClass:
    @allure.step("Title")
    def some_method(self):
        pass
SomeClass().some_method()
```

Now allure report for this code will contain a step with name "Title" and with 1 parameter `self`.

#### What is the expected behavior?

I believe that report must contain the step without parameter `self` by default.
I offer to implement optional parameter if you like to see `self` in parameters list.

```python
@allure.step("Title", show_self=True)
```

#### What is the motivation / use case for changing the behavior?

In most cases there is no need to see `self` in parameters list.

issue_title: Flash without resetting the board
issue_body:
Add special `--no-reset` parameter for `mbedflash flash` sub-command, which will skip touching the serial port. This is preferred by daemon, because then it can keep serial port open and skip some error handling.

I do not think it is really necessary to [reset](https://github.com/ARMmbed/mbed-flasher/blob/master/mbed_flasher/flashers/FlasherMbed.py#L192) the board before flashing... DAPlink should do what is necessary. Or is it not so?

Daemon will reset the board after flasher is done. It might be an important feature to support e.g. power consumption profiling in early boot. We flash first and leave the target halted, then daemon launches power meter recording and unlocks the board's MCU.

issue_body:
The pymatgen powder XRD pattern could be used as a structure descriptor.

- possibly smooth it first or add an option to smooth
- rather than taking discrete values, might want to integrate the value in a bin. e.g., 0-1 degree, 1-2 degree, etc.

Not sure how well it would work, but at least an option to try.

```

Then the SCP transfer would fail. 

It would fail to transfer the file. Debugging on router side indicated the SCP communication started, but ended when I would expect the file to be transferred.

Increasing the SCP timeout from 5 seconds to 30 seconds did not help. It doesn't look like the transfer even starts.

Manual transfer of same file from server to router works using SCP:

```
scp cisco1.txt.original user@10.10.10.10:flash:/cisco1.txt
```


### Second Issue
Same router would not let me to a configure replace where I removed the self-signed certificate. In other words, I had a perfectly valid config file that removed the self-signed certificate. I would get a message complaining about 'no quit' (i.e. the end of the self-signed certificate). I had to manually remove it and then do the configure replace operation.

### Third Issue
The self-signed certificate contains a tab in the quit line and must be structured exactly this way. You cannot just copy and paste it (as the tab will get converted to spaces).

---

Moved from https://github.com/napalm-automation/napalm-ios/issues/143
Partially solved in https://github.com/napalm-automation/napalm/pull/381

issue_title: How to use folium with iPython / Jupyter notebook
issue_body:
As far as I know there are two approaches for using folium inside a notebook:
- use folium.initialize_notebook()
- use inline_map / iframe.

Several bugs are associated to the first (a quick search listed #90 , #44 , #132 ). Is there any advantage in using the first approach over the second? I tried to look for an answer in the issue tracker but did not find any.

issue_title: Schema without attributes can not be deserialized due to missing attributes key.
issue_body:
When I define my schema

```python
class MyFirstSchema(Schema):
    id = fields.Int()

    class Meta:
        type_ = 'firsts'
```

and then try to load it like this

```python
data = {'data': {'id': 1, 'type': 'firsts'}}
MyFirstSchema().load(data)


```

I get the following error:

```
marshmallow.exceptions.ValidationError: `data` object must include `attributes` key.
```

I do, however, not find any specification in JSON API saying that the `attributes` key has to be present.

Here is a full example:
```python
from marshmallow_jsonapi import Schema, fields

class MyFirstSchema(Schema):
    id = fields.Int()

    class Meta:
        type_ = 'firsts'

data = {'data': {'id': 1, 'type': 'firsts'}}
MyFirstSchema().load(data)
```

issue_title: _before_* callbacks ignores file deletion
issue_body:
When the model has defined  `autodelete=True,` and the callback retirns `True` the register in the dal will not be deleted, but the files in `uploads/` desapears.

**Model**
```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-


def pprint(*args): print args
def bkp_delete( s ):
#    pprint(s) #.select()[0]
    return True    

db.define_table('img',
    Field('image',
        'upload',
        required=True,
        notnull=True,
        autodelete=True,
        uploadseparate=True),
    Field('mdate', 'datetime', notnull=True),
    Field('landmark', 'boolean'))

db.img._before_delete.append( lambda s: bkp_delete(s) ) 
```

**Controller**
```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-


def index(): 
    return dict(message="hello from img.py")

def add():
    form = SQLFORM(db.img).process()
    return dict(f=form)

def grid():
    grid=SQLFORM.grid(db.img, user_signature=False)
    return dict(grid=grid)

# add at least 2 registers
# delete 1
# go to de grid, you will see the 2registers.
# try to see the attachment
```

issue_title: Pinning of ruamel.yaml versions?
issue_body:
Recent published versions of [ruamel.yaml](https://bitbucket.org/ruamel/yaml) have introduced breaking changes for chartpress. See:
- https://github.com/jupyterhub/chartpress/pull/17
- https://github.com/jupyterhub/chartpress/issues/19
- https://github.com/jupyterhub/zero-to-jupyterhub-k8s/issues/831

To avoid this in the future, it might be a good idea to pin the version of `ruamel.yaml` to be used by chartpress. We can also use a narrow range of versions known to work.

issue_title: How should we handle types that are global_allow=False in queries in collections? (Plone 4.3.x)
issue_body:
I'm posting this issue here and in plone.formwidget.querystring, feel free to close if inappropriate.

We're using https://github.com/collective/collective.nitf instead of Plone's "News Item" content type, and decided to set "Implicitly addable" (`global_allow`) to `False` in "News Item" `portal_types`. For the sake of usability/familiarity of the user with Plone naming, we decided to rename in a policy product the nitf items to "News Item" as if it was Plone's. So far, so good.

But now we're having some usability problems when adding a collection, since both "News Item" are being shown in the select field (In Brazilian portuguese, "Noticia" is the same as "News"):

![selecao_008](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8203476/22159433/06069f5a-df28-11e6-808e-1c0818348a85.png)

I know that from a programatic POV this is correct (they are both valid in `portal_types`), but not from an user perspective: if I can't add the "News Item" anywhere in the site since I set "Implicity addable" to false, why it's being shown here?

I'm not familiar with the code, but what defines what types should be shown in the select? Is it possible to add somewhere to not use items which `global_allow` is `False`, or to take into consideration `types_not_searched` in `portal_properties`? I tried to add "News Item" to `types_not_searched` but it didn't work.

issue_title: parser: query parser does not parse all possible combinations of NOT operator correctly
issue_body:
The `NOT` operator is not parsed as NotOp.

OR NOT
-----------
`NOT` is parsed as a `Value`.

**Example:**
`test AND (author:"Alexander, S" OR NOT author:"Costa, M S")`

gives

`AndOp(ValueQuery(Value('test')), AndOp(OrOp(KeywordOp(Keyword('author'), DoubleQuotedValue('Alexander, S')), ValueQuery(Value('NOT'))), KeywordOp(Keyword('author'), DoubleQuotedValue('Costa, M S'))))`

AND (NOT ...
-----------------
`NOT` is parsed as a `Value`.

**Example:**
`test AND (NOT author:"Alexander, S")`

gives

`AndOp(ValueQuery(Value('test')), AndOp(ValueQuery(Value('NOT')), KeywordOp(Keyword('author'), DoubleQuotedValue('Alexander, S'))))`

Only AND NOT works
-----------------------------
NOT is then properly parsed as NotOp

**Example:**

`test AND (author:"Alexander, S" AND NOT author:"Costa, M S")`

gives

`AndOp(ValueQuery(Value('test')), AndOp(KeywordOp(Keyword('author'), DoubleQuotedValue('Alexander, S')), NotOp(KeywordOp(Keyword('author'), DoubleQuotedValue('Costa, M S')))))`


If some operations are not supported like `OR NOT`, then server should return an error. It should not parse it sometime as a `Value` and sometime as `NotOp`.

previously reported as inveniosoftware/invenio#3366

issue_title: How do I configure arguments for a given hook?
issue_body:
Apologies if the question looks too simple. I'm new to pre-commit. 

I followed these steps to set things up:
```
brew install pre-commit
cd my_repo
pre-commit install
```
and then created a `.pre-commit-config.yaml` file inside `my_repo` with the following contents:

```
-   repo: git://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    sha: v1.2.0  # Use the ref you want to point at
    hooks:
    -   id: check-added-large-files
```

My understanding is that `check-added-large-files` supports arguments `args: ['--maxkb=123']`. 

I would like to set up the hook so that it prevents me from committing files larger than 50MB *any time* I try to git commit anything on the repo. Where exactly do I specify these arguments?

issue_title: Issues with Implementing Machine Comprehension
issue_body:
I want to create a python script that takes a "passage" and a "question" and returns a string that contains the answer (or at least the location of the answer). I have been running into several errors and I have come to the point where I think I have run out of help material to look into and research. I am using the pre-trained model and I have formatted the passage and question as Dict[str, tensor.LongTensor], which are the required parameters for the forward method in allennlp.models.reading_comprehension.

issue_title: test_install fails after following some of our instructions
issue_body:
1.  If you follow the instructions for installing AllenNLP via pip, any tests that rely on `scripts` will fail (although I think we've taken care of these) and the tests that rely on the JVM may fail.

2.  If you follow the instructions for using AllenNLP from Docker going forward (as we're starting to package AllenNLP via pip) the user gets 12 failures.

```
$ docker run -it -p 8000:8000 --rm allennlp/allennlp:v0.5.1-pip test-install
...
tests/models/semantic_parsing/wikitables/wikitables_erm_semantic_parser_test.py F
tests/models/semantic_parsing/wikitables/wikitables_mml_semantic_parser_test.py FFFFFFFF
tests/predictors/wikitables_parser_test.py FF
tests/training/metrics/wikitables_accuracy_test.py F
...
```

Ideally in either of these cases, `test_install` would validate the installation.

issue_body: There are plenty of places in the code where we do `Variable(variable.data.new().resize_(some_shape).fill_(some_value))`, as that is the recommended way to create a new variable on the same device as some other variable.  This is an annoying pattern to see in code and to remember when writing code.  We should add a method to `nn.util` that does this in a configurable way, and replace all places in the code where we do this with the new method.

issue_title: Wrong resolution upon 2nd pipenv install
issue_body:
##### Describe your environment

1. macOS 10.12.6
1. Python version: any python version, bug seen with 2.7 and 3.6
1. Pipenv version: 9.0.0

##### Expected result

Expecting `pipenv install` to yield same result consistently, in this particular case `setuptools 33.1.1` should be resolved (due to pex constraint)

##### Actual result

pipenv resolves `setuptools 33.1.1` on 1st installation, but not on 2nd

##### Steps to replicate

You can reproduce like so (in any folder, using a scratch one here).

First time around, everything looks good (pex wants `setuptools<34`), note the minor annoying question **"Did you mean `pexpect`?"**:

```
pipenv --rm ; rm -f Pipfile* ; pipenv install pex ; pipenv install pytest --dev ; pipenv graph
...
Did you mean pexpect? [Y/n]: n
Installing pex…
...
pex==1.2.15
  - setuptools [required: >=20.3,<34.0, installed: 33.1.1]
...
pytest==3.3.1
  - setuptools [required: Any, installed: 33.1.1]
...
```

Now delete venv and install again -> wrong setuptools is resolved:

```
pipenv --rm ; pipenv install ; pipenv graph
...
pex==1.2.15
  - setuptools [required: >=20.3,<34.0, installed: 38.2.4]
...
pytest==3.3.1
  - setuptools [required: Any, installed: 38.2.4]
...
```

Also note that `pipenv install` acted here as-if I state `--dev`... wasn't expecting `pytest` to be installed here (was expecting it to be installed if I stated `--dev`).

issue_title: Virtualenv created includeas copy of python exectuable
issue_body:

issue_title: Prompt the user before creating a virtualenv
issue_body:
Be sure to check the existing issues, both open and closed.

If you are in a directory not associated with a virtualenv, `pipenv install` will create it. While it's a great feature, I noticed during numerous training I gave that it bites the beginners more often than not:

- they often pipenv install from the wrong directory by mistake
- they will install the wrong python version

I'm suggesting that if pipenv is run in a directory not associated with a virtualenv, then it should not immediatly proceed. It should pause, and prompt the user with something like:
```
You are currently in:
/stuff/thing
But this directory is not associated with a virtualenv yet. What do you want to do:
[1] Create a virtualenv with Python x.w
[2] Create a virtualenv with Python x.x
[3] Create a virtualenv with Python y.y
[4] Create a virtualenv with Python y.x
[5] Abort.
```
This would have several benefits:

- Limit creation by mistake in the wrong directory. 
- Allow the user to know that there are several Python executables available and that he/she can choose.

Of course, this prompt should be disabled with an option such as --no-prompt for automation.

issue_title: incompatible changes in 3.9.2
issue_body:
With 70a7b5a16748afbf0d48ca180c2b7613fdd7e7d0 we introduced some  backwards incompatible changes:

 * `Component.is_broken` got replaced with `Component.errors`
 * events with a `RDATE;VALUE=PERIOD:19970101T180000Z/19970102T070000Z,19970109T180000Z/PT5H30M` component still had an `RDATE` with `VALUE=PERIOD` param before, now they are `RDATE:None`

While I do agree with both changes, I think they should have been deferred to the 4.0.0 release. Because we don't have `VALUE=PERIOD` anyway I think we can leave this one as it is, but I believe we should bring back `Component.is_broken` for the 3.9.3 release.

issue_title: Review Nix-setup
issue_body:
@johbo Since also you've been using pip2nix to help Python development at work, would you be interested in and able to review and discuss about the pip2nix based generic/reusable Nix environment in this project. I try hard to support best practices for both traditional Python development and Nix packaging. I'm planning to blog about it in detail later:

* ``./setup.py`` is mostly empty and everything, including setup_requires, install_requires, test_requires, is in declarative ./setup.cfg (supported by current setuptools)
* ``./requirements.txt`` includes everything from setup.cfg, but also QA tools like flake8 used indevelopment
* ``./nix-support/requirements.nix`` is pip2nix -generated from requirements.txt
* ``./nix-support/default.nix`` includes generic helpers build from the above
  * ``setup`` is setup.cfg parsed into nix language
  * ``pythonPackages`` is the final pythonPackages extended with requirements.nix + overrides.nix
  * ``env`` builds python.withPackages to provide interpreter with requirements.txt e.g. for PyCharm
  * ``shell`` is convenient shell shortcut for the above env usable with ``nix-shell nix-support -A shell``
  * ``pip2nix`` provides shell with pip2nix to allow easy update of requirements.nix
* ``./default.nix`` is generic buildPythonPackage derivation for building the developed python Package (and to provide development shell for that; it uses parsed setup.cfg to populate name, buildInputs and propagatedBuildInputs).
* ``./release.nix`` provides optional Hydra build targets
