issue_title: Enabled logging eats exception backtraces
issue_body:
When the default ApplicationRunner  enables logging via `txaio.start_logging()` backtraces raised in asyncio code are not printed anymore. Only the warning itself is visible.

Consider the following snippet:
```
import logging
from os import environ

from autobahn.asyncio.wamp import ApplicationSession, ApplicationRunner


class MyComponent(ApplicationSession):
    async def onJoin(self, details):
        logging.info('We want a backtrace!')

        raise RuntimeError('meh')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    runner = ApplicationRunner(
        environ.get("AUTOBAHN_DEMO_ROUTER", u"ws://127.0.0.1:8080/ws"),
        u"realm1",
    )
    runner.run(MyComponent)
```

Output with the unmodified ApplicationRunner looks like this:

```
2017-04-27T11:53:11 Task exception was never retrieved
future: <Task finished coro=<onJoin() done, defined at break_it.py:20> exception=RuntimeError('meh',)>
```

Is this expected? It makes debugging problems kind of impossible.
 
When I remove the call to `txaio.start_logging()` I get what I expect:

```
ERROR:asyncio:Task exception was never retrieved
future: <Task finished coro=<onJoin() done, defined at break_it.py:20> exception=RuntimeError('meh',)>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/asyncio/tasks.py", line 239, in _step
    result = coro.send(None)
  File "break_it.py", line 23, in onJoin
    raise RuntimeError('meh')
RuntimeError: meh
```

issue_title: Shape issue with data
issue_body:
Hello,

In my dataset, I am given in the input is  a set of action sequences, each of which are executed with a different objective or goal in mind (example: if a person wants to transport an object from one place to another, they perform a sequence of actions which consists of 'initial state->lift(object, person)->load(object, car)-> lift(..) -> drive(...)-> load(...)->drop(...)->goal'). This sequence of actions constitutes a single trace for me. Each action can, however be applied in specific world states (i.e. the action 'load' can only execute when there is a car to carry it in; i.e. predicate 'isCar' is true; these predicates are otherwise called preconditions) and on execution result in a different world state (e.g. after the action 'load', the box has been loaded into the car; i.e. predicate 'boxInCar' is true; these predicates are otherwise called effects). My task here is given these traces of action sequences, to 'learn'  the underlying action model i.e. blueprint of preconditions and effects for each action. Example of an action model for action 'load' can be:

preconditions: isCar(), isDriver(),isCarRunning().. etc.
effects: onCar(), carMoving().. etc.

In tensorflow, assuming that I have 4 actions to learn, I am representing each action of each trace in the form of a sequence of 16 dimensional input vector representing each action. Here I construct a superset of all possible action models (i.e. all possible permutations of predicates representing preconditions and effects), and wish to test them one by one to see which is more accurate.

Input Vector (assuming action1 is executed):
[action1 (1)
action2 (0)
action3 (0)
action4 (0)
(preconditions of action1) (1)
(effects of action1) (1)

.
(preconditions of action2) (0)
(effects of action2) (0)
.
(preconditions of action3) (0)
(effects of action3) (0)
.
(preconditions of action4) (0)
(effects of action4) (0)
.
]

and trying to predict the next action in the sequence in a one-hot encoded fashion:
Output Vector:
[first_action (0)
secondAction (1)
ThirdAction (0)
ForthAction (1)]

 This vector is 16 dimensional (4 rows for each of the actions, rest 12 for each of the preconditions and effects of all (3 per action, thus 12 in all) ). Each time an action is executed, the vector position corresponding to the action name is set to 1, and the positions corresponding to its preconditions and effects is set to 1 as well. The rest are kept at 0.


My code is as follows:

`if __name__ == '__main__':
    #code to add padded data
    train_input, train_output, maxTraceLength = reader_pddlCodeIssueRep.loadData()
    #80% of examples in training, 20% for testing
    train_input, test_input = train_input[:int(len(train_input)*0.8)], train_input[int(len(train_input)*0.8):]
    print('len(train_input)','len(train_input)',len(train_input), len(test_input))
    train_output, test_output = train_output[:int(len(train_output)*0.8)], train_output[int(len(train_output)*0.8):] #everything beyond 10,000
    print('len(train_output)','len(test_output)',len(train_output), len(test_output))
    
    # Network building
    net = tflearn.input_data([None, 1, 16])
    net = tflearn.lstm(net, 128, dropout=0.8)
    net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, 4, activation='softmax')
    net = tflearn.regression(net, optimizer='adam', learning_rate=0.001,
                             loss='categorical_crossentropy')
    # Training
    model = tflearn.DNN(net, tensorboard_verbose=0)
    model.fit(train_input, train_output, validation_set=(test_input, test_output), show_metric=True,
              batch_size=maxTraceLength)

`
Also attached here:
[codeIssueRep.txt](https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/files/544475/codeIssueRep.txt)

Issue: I seem to be falling prey to a shape issue which says `ValueError: Cannot feed value of shape (13, 16) for Tensor 'InputData/X:0', which has shape '(?, 1, 16)'`. 

I am a relative newbie at tensorflow and tflearn and don't have too much of ML experience behind me. I have read similar issues in the tflearn issue page (thanks to @aymericdamien for his answers, they have been very useful in clearing doubts that I have had )and ones on TensorFlow too, and feel that I am representing it correctly but my data might not be in the correct format somehow.

Lastly, sorry for the long post, I just tried to clarify my problem in general to give better context :)
Would be very obliged if someone could help!

issue_title: Deprecate/remove reader.schema

issue_title: query and querymany methods give different answers
issue_body:
Consider the following two Python scripts `querymany.py`:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python

import sys
import mygene

mg = mygene.MyGeneInfo()

genes = []
for line in sys.stdin:
    genes.append(line.strip())

results = mg.querymany(genes, scopes="symbol", fields=["ensembl"], species="human", verbose=False)

for res in results:
    hgnc_name = res["query"]
    if "ensembl" in res and "gene" in res["ensembl"]:
        sys.stdout.write("%s\t%s\n" % (hgnc_name, res["ensembl"]["gene"]))
```

And `query.py`:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                

import sys
import mygene

mg = mygene.MyGeneInfo()

genes = []
for line in sys.stdin:
    genes.append(line.strip())

for gene in genes:
    result = mg.query(gene, scopes="symbol", fields=["ensembl"], species="human", verbose=False)
    hgnc_name = gene
    for hit in result["hits"]:
        if "ensembl" in hit and "gene" in hit["ensembl"]:
            sys.stdout.write("%s\t%s\n" % (hgnc_name, hit["ensembl"]["gene"]))
```

Both scripts take in a list of gene names from standard input, query them for Ensembl ENSG-prefixed gene names, and write the ID-mapping results to standard output.

The issue is that `querymany` and `query` seem to return two different answers.

Sample `genes.txt` list:

```
$ more genes.txt
DDX26B
CCDC83
MAST3
RPL11
ZDHHC20
LUC7L3
SNORD49A
CTSH
ACOT8
```

Then the result from `querymany`:

```
$ ./querymany.py < genes.txt
CCDC83  ENSG00000150676
MAST3   ENSG00000099308
RPL11   ENSG00000142676
ZDHHC20 ENSG00000180776
LUC7L3  ENSG00000108848
SNORD49A        ENSG00000277370
CTSH    ENSG00000103811
ACOT8   ENSG00000101473
```

And the result from `query`:

```
$ ./query.py < genes.txt
DDX26B  ENSG00000225235
DDX26B  ENSG00000165359
CCDC83  ENSG00000150676
MAST3   ENSG00000099308
RPL11   ENSG00000142676
ZDHHC20 ENSG00000180776
ZDHHC20 ENSG00000236953
LUC7L3  ENSG00000108848
SNORD49A        ENSG00000277370
CTSH    ENSG00000103811
ACOT8   ENSG00000101473
```

The difference between output from these tests:

```
$ diff ../results/query.txt ../results/querymany.txt 
1,2d0
< DDX26B        ENSG00000225235
< DDX26B        ENSG00000165359
7d4
< ZDHHC20       ENSG00000236953
```

The `mygene` library was installed via `pip`:

```
$ pip show mygene
Name: mygene
Version: 3.0.0
Summary: Python Client for MyGene.Info services.
Home-page: https://github.com/suLab/mygene.py
Author: Chunlei Wu, Cyrus Afrasiabi, Sebastien Lelong
Author-email: cwu@scripps.edu
License: BSD
Location: /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages
Requires: requests
```

I'm running Python 2.7.5:

```
$ python --version
Python 2.7.5
```

issue_title: Not passing get params to controller function
issue_body:
I have a swagger endpoint like this:

```
paths:
  /query:
    get:
      summary: Query material's information
      description: |
        This interface using application token to authorize a client, and return the material's information under specified orderId.
      parameters:
        - name: token
          in: query
          description: Api token of the client.
          required: true
          type: string
        - name: order_id
          in: query
          description: Target orderId.
          required: true
          type: number
          format: int64
      tags:
        - Query
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Material informations.
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/MaterialInfo'
        '401':
          description: Invalid token.
        default:
          description: Unexpected error
          schema:
            $ref: '#/definitions/Error'
```
And I have used swagger editor to generate a server, I got two _.py_ files:
**app.py:**
```
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import connexion

if __name__ == '__main__':
   app = connexion.App(__name__, 8889, specification_dir='./swagger/')
   app.add_api('swagger.yaml', arguments={'title': 'Server-to-server api for querying material information of PLUS platform.'})
   app.run()
```
**default_controller.py:**
```

def query_get(token, orderId) -> str:
   return 'do some magic!'
```
It looks fine.
I have tried using some wrong params, it returns a good 400 status with error information.


But when I using correct params, here comes **500** with no details information.
I have read the documents and connexion-examples, it looks like nothing wrong with this generated code above.



And after reading the connexion's code and debugging, I found that **connexion call _query_get_ with no arguments.**
There was an "_Exception: query_get() missing 2 required positional arguments: 'token' and 'orderId' _" raised.

I changed **default_controller.py** to:
```
from flask import request


def query_get():
    token = request.args.get("token")
    orderId = request.args.get("order_id")
    return "ok"
```
Then this endpoint works fine.


In this case, connexion verified the params but forget to pass it to controller function.

(PS: I'm using python 3.4.3 with connexion 1.0.49)

issue_title: Music, Instruments, Music software icons
issue_body:
I did some research on requests for music icons. There's a lot, and I'm going to reference the ones I came across. I'm currently working for a music festival, for which we have 3 different websites (and more coming), all of them using FontAwesome. But I'm also a musician and would like to make apps / websites for musicians / about music, etc. Would be sweet if we had music icons.

I think it would benefit sites on musicians, bands, DJs, producers, events, festivals, studios, audio software, music web apps, etc.

issue_title: [global] section scope
issue_body:
Separated from #802 

This scope issue with `[global]` often bothers me. What I usually do in research is to prototype using some local cells with loose SoS statements, and after my codes are tested I put them to formal steps to execute. This inevitably involves using global variables in both inside and outside steps. With the current SoS implemention I usually find myself having to define one same variable twice: one under [global] to let workflows use them, one as a local statement for interactive analysis. It would be great if we can make this part easier.

issue_title: [local] section
issue_body:
Not sure if this is a good idea but I found myself doing this

```
---
%sosrun BIG
[global]
[BIG]
sos_run('small')
---
%run small
[???]
statement
[small]
---
```

The idea is that I have cells for small tasks, basically steps that I would like to experiment or run interactively, then I would like to run many such steps in `BIG` which has a bunch of `sos_run` calls. The problem here is that `statement` in `small` is supposed to be local, only used for `%run`, and I do not want to put it in `[global]` which would be merged to the global section. However, if I do not use `[global]` and put the statement directly there, the whole cell would be excluded from the notebook workflow because only cells with `[]` would be counted as part of the workflow.

Solution 1:
Define `[local]` section that would be excluded from the global workflow.

Solution 2:
Allow the use of 
```
statement
[step]
...
```
and use another method to decide if `step` should be part of the notebook workflow.

issue_title: Ability to terminate a SAS session from within an ipynb?
issue_body:
Hi.  I love the saspy package.  It is a great addition to the SAS System.  I have searched the docs and can't find a way to terminate a SAS session from within an ipynb.  I found the article about disconnecting from a network and then reconnecting to a running SAS session.  That is very useful.   But, it would also be useful to be able to TERMINATE a connection to a remote SAS server from within an ipynb.  I am asking since we have seen instances where stopping the Python kernel, and closing the ipynb does NOT terminate the running remote SAS session on our Linux SAS GRID.

Are there any plans to add a new command to the saspy package that would allow us to terminate a remote SAS session?  Or, perhaps this already exists and I've just missed it?  ;-)

issue_title: selectEdge Event is not fired
Atomicpuppy not reading correctly from category based projections
issue_body:
When consuming events from category projections atomic puppy does not track the correct stream. 

Entries from eventstore contains two fields for the event number, one that makes reference to the original stream it was published (called `eventNumber`) and another that makes reference to the stream that is being queried (called `positionEventNumber`).

For example let's consider events that are published to the `order-{order_id}` stream and we read from the `$ce-order` stream. Let's assume and order which id 456 is placed and an event order_placed event is raised in the `order-456` stream. When reading from the `$ce-order` stream, the eventNumber field will be 0 (since is the first event published in the `order-456` stream, but it may be the third event collected in the `$ce-order` stream (because other events about other orders where published) and this the field positionEventNumber contains the correct number that is 2.

Same thing happen with the fields streamId and positionStreamId.

Extract from the json response:
```
  "entries": [
    {
      "eventId": "5f1bd258-e690-4f7c-b3dd-b46cf384871e",
      "eventType": "order_placed",
      "eventNumber": 0,
      "data": "{    \"order_id\": \"456\"}",
      "streamId": "order-456",
      "isJson": true,
      "isMetaData": false,
      "isLinkMetaData": false,
      "positionEventNumber": 2,
      "positionStreamId": "$ce-order",
      "title": "0@order-456",
      "id": "http://localhost:2113/streams/order-456/0",
      "updated": "2016-04-08T11:07:21.083654Z",
      "author": {
        "name": "EventStore"
      },
      "summary": "$>",
      "links": [
        {
          "uri": "http://localhost:2113/streams/order-456/0",
          "relation": "edit"
        },
        {
          "uri": "http://localhost:2113/streams/order-456/0",
          "relation": "alternate"
        }
      ]
    },
....
```

issue_title: Specify a channel in which to search for dependencies during build
issue_body:
I am trying to integrate travis-ci with a [project on which I'm working](http://github.com/jrsmith3/ibei/). Travis-ci is failing at the build step of the package with the [error](https://travis-ci.org/jrsmith3/ibei/jobs/55853116#L684)

```
$ conda build -c jrsmith3 conda.recipe
There was an error importing jinja2.
Please run `conda install jinja2` to enable jinja template support
Removing old build directory
Removing old work directory
BUILD START: ibei--np19py27_0
Fetching package metadata: ........
Error: No packages found in current linux-64 channels matching: physicalproperty
The command "conda build -c jrsmith3 conda.recipe" failed and exited with 1 during .
Your build has been stopped.
```

I think the problem is that one of the dependencies of the package under test is only available from my binstar channel. As you can see in the snippet above, I'm calling the build command with the "-c jrsmith3" flag, so I think `conda` should be searching my binstar channel for dependencies.

I don't understand why travis-ci can't build my package. Is there anything I am missing?


[`.travis.yml`](https://github.com/jrsmith3/ibei/blob/cd68b9d47c1770cc9840dafae9618b25b9d1c41b/.travis.yml):

```yaml
language: python
python:
  # We don't actually use the Travis Python, but this keeps it organized.
  - "2.6"
  - "2.7"
  - "3.3"
  - "3.4"
install:
  - sudo apt-get update
  # You may want to periodically update this, although the conda update
  # conda line below will keep everything up-to-date.  We do this
  # conditionally because it saves us some downloading if the version is
  # the same.
  - if [[ "$TRAVIS_PYTHON_VERSION" == "2.7" ]]; then
      wget http://repo.continuum.io/miniconda/Miniconda-3.4.2-Linux-x86_64.sh -O miniconda.sh;
    else
      wget http://repo.continuum.io/miniconda/Miniconda3-3.4.2-Linux-x86_64.sh -O miniconda.sh;
    fi
  - bash miniconda.sh -b -p $HOME/miniconda
  - export PATH="$HOME/miniconda/bin:$PATH"
  - hash -r
  - conda config --set always_yes yes --set changeps1 no
  - conda update -q conda
  - conda install conda-build
  # Useful for debugging any issues with conda
  - conda info -a

  # Replace dep1 dep2 ... with your dependencies
  - conda create -q -n test-environment python=$TRAVIS_PYTHON_VERSION anaconda
  - source activate test-environment
  - conda build -c jrsmith3 conda.recipe
  - conda install ibei --use-local

script: nosetests
```


The package's [`meta.yaml`](https://github.com/jrsmith3/ibei/blob/cd68b9d47c1770cc9840dafae9618b25b9d1c41b/conda.recipe/meta.yaml):

```yaml
package:
    name: ibei
    # version: x.y.z
    # The version string is stored in `ibei/version.py`.
    # `build.sh` includes code to properly incorporate the version
    # string in the built package.

source:
    path: ..

requirements:
    build:
        - python
        - numpy
        - astropy
        - sympy
        - physicalproperty
    run:
        - python
        - numpy
        - astropy
        - sympy
        - physicalproperty

test:
    files:
        - ../test/test_DeVosSolarcell.py
        - ../test/test_SBSolarcell.py
        - ../test/test_uiebi.py
    requires:
        - nose

about:
    home: https://github.com/jrsmith3/ibei
    license: MIT
    summary: Calculator for incomplete Bose-Einstein integral
```

issue_title: Discussion: Problems/Solutions when using conda for reproducible environments.
issue_body:
I'd like to open a discussion about issues that need to be solved to make conda a real solution for producing reproducible environments.

A few of the issues that I noticed are:

1. Different conda versions have different package resolution strategies. Because of this it can happen that an `environment.yml` file stops working with a newer conda version. This might be solved by including the conda version in the YAML file. But AFAIK it is not possible to have multiple conda's in one miniconda/anaconda installation.
2. It seems that older conda versions can not be used after some time since the package format is still changing. See e.g. https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/1642 . But this might only apply to new packages so please correct me if that's wrong.
3. Conda and conda-env have to work together and work with channels. This is already covered in #2800 and other issues. 

This is related to a discussion started in https://github.com/conda-forge/openblas-feedstock/issues/10

@ocefpaf and others, please add other problems or solutions to my issues in this thread.

issue_title: Create .condarc file in Windows
issue_body:
Hi

I have been trying to setup a .condarc file in Windows specifically for proxy_servers keys as suggested [here](http://conda.pydata.org/docs/config.html#configure-conda-for-use-behind-a-proxy-server-proxy-servers). One thing I figured out is that unlike Linux ( in which filenames starting with a dot is a convention for hidden files), starting a filename with dot does not do anything, so what I did was to create a text file with  
```
proxy_servers:
         http: http://19.12.2.105:83
```
and name it as .condarc without any extension, then put it in my root directory and anaconda directory and make them hidden. The result of running
```
conda info

issue_title: 'unsubscribe' / 'unregister' leak persistent references
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/330000, @ctheune  reports:

> See zope.interface.adapter.BaseAdapterRegistry. The methods `unregister` and `unsubscribe` leave the attributes `_adapters` and `_subscribers` with an interface reference in their dictionary key although the value indicates an empty value and thus the key could be removed.

> For persistent registries this causes the database to reference interfaces even after all registrations for those interfaces have been removed.

issue_title: Add force_stepk/force_maxk/ii_pad_factor kwargs to PhaseScreenPSF and OpticalPSF.
issue_body:
`PhaseScreenPSF` currently accepts `_force_stepk` and `_force_maxk` kwargs to forward to `InterpolatedImage`.  `OpticalPSF` is really just a wrapper around `PhaseScreenPSF`, so it should get these kwargs too.

Both `PhaseScreenPSF` and `OpticalPSF` also accept a `pad_factor` kwarg which governs the transformation from pupil space to real space.  It would also be nice to be able to regulate the real space -> Fourier space transformation by forwarding a `pad_factor` kwarg to `InterpolatedImage`.  I *think* these two `pad_factor`s should be distinct, so I'm inclined to call the kwargs that gets forwarded from `PhaseScreenPSF` and `OpticalPSF` to `InterpolatedImage` `ii_pad_factor` or something like that.

issue_title: COMM_KA vrs RELAY_{tx|rx} spec issue/question
issue_body:
Hmm - There seems to be a large timing issue that manifests in multiple ways, no one way is clearly the problem.  This could also be due to the way the Android Commissioning app is structured :-(

For the purposes of this discussion, I want to divide the commissioning process into 3 phases.

Phase 1 - is prior to the DISCOVERY REQUEST
Phase 2 - is during the time the RLY_{TX|RX} is active
Phase 3 - is after JOIN_FIN

During Phase 1 - the COMM_KA(keep alive) is a very useful message to determine if the application should shut down, or give up - etc - this make sense.

During Phase 2 - (when RLY_{TX|RX} is active) the COMM_KA(keepalive) becomes very problematic, and often does not occur at an actual rate that it needs to.

This also has to do with TIMEOUT_COMM_PET (50secs) and TIMEOUT_LEAD_PET(50secs), THR_DTLS_INIT_RETRANSMIT_TIMEOUT(8sec start), note the RE-TRANSMIT timeout increases exponentially.

In the android app (current design), there is a thread that does the keep alive process. This thread does not actually send the COMM_KA over the air (wifi), instead it queues the request into a work queue, another thread takes the COMM_KA  {and the RLY_TXRX messages} and sends these via the DTLS session to the AGENT.

The actual transmission time of the COMM_KA can be upwards of 20 seconds after the request is queued. Why so long? Because the under powered joiner the DTLS session with the JOINER is takes more time, in addition if there are *ANY* over the air re-transmissions of RF packets, it takes longer for the *LARGE* packets to be reassembled. 

In effect, the COMM_KA and RELAY_{TX|RX} are serialized and msg(A) slows down msg(B) because the messages are processed in order.  This often brings us just past the TIMEOUTs,  in any of the 4 parties involved (AndroidApp,   NCP,  JOINING_ROUTER,  and JOINING_DEVICE) it is not consistent, and not reliable.

One PROPOSED change - is to treat the RELAY_{TX|RX} activity as an alternate COMM_KA, the rational is: If the RELAY_TX & RX is occurring, this is occurring with the Border Agent, and is being sent by the COMMISSIONER - if those messages are succeeding (making forward progress in any form) then the commissioner has not walked away.

Are there any component lifecycle methods or events that we can utilize to customize the behaviour of the widget _after_ it has been mounted on the DOM? issue_title: Use libdeepspeech in DeepSpeech.py and remove duplicate code
issue_body: Part of libdeepspeech is a native implementation of the processing done in audiofile_to_input_vector (as well as the reimplementation of python_speech_features). We should use the Python bindings for this library instead, partly because it's considerably faster than the equivalent Python/python_speech_features code, but mostly so we don't have two copies of the same thing which could possibly diverge.

issue_title: How to get a refresh_token?
issue_body:
Hi,

I'd like to get the `refresh_token` for the first time only so the app doesn't require subsequent authentication prompts (as this will be a script running in the background). 

It works the first time but after the token expires it gives an error:

```
 gauth.Refresh()
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pydrive/auth.py", line 369, in Refresh
    raise RefreshError('No refresh_token found.'
pydrive.auth.RefreshError: No refresh_token found.Please set access_type of OAuth to offline.
```

This is the code handling the authentication:
```python
# Sort out the authentication...
gauth = GoogleAuth()

# Try to load saved client credentials
gauth.LoadCredentialsFile(os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)) + os.sep + "credentials.json")

if gauth.credentials is None:
    # Authenticate if they're not there
    gauth.LocalWebserverAuth()
elif gauth.access_token_expired:
    # Refresh them if expired
    gauth.Refresh()
else:
    # Initialize the saved creds
    gauth.Authorize()
```

Then I have a `settings.yaml`:

```yaml
client_config_backend: settings
client_config:
  client_id: xxx
  client_secret: xxx

save_credentials: True
save_credentials_backend: file
save_credentials_file: <path_to_save_credentials.json>

get_refresh_token: True

oauth_scope:
  - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file
  - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.install

Any ideas why `refresh_token` is always `NULL` in the response `credentials.json`?

issue_title: Accept translations in any language
issue_body:
I'm working on a project which requires localized fields to be in at least one language, but this could be any language. This use case is provided out of the box by `django.forms.fields.MultiValueField` by combining `required=True` and `require_all_fields=False`. django-localized-fields makes this hard though:

- If the field form is given `required=True`, it marks the default language as mandatory in `localized_fields.forms.LocalizedFieldForm.__init__`.

- If the field is given `null=False` (which makes sense if you are expecting translations in at least one language) it checks that the default language has a translation in `localized_fields.fields.field.LocalizedField.validate`.

I think this introduces an arbitrary limitation. If the feature of marking some concrete translations as required is needed, the form field could just accept a list with the required languages, `required=True` would then just make the root multi value form field required (which means that any translation would suffice) and if `null=False` the field would just validate that at least one translation exists.

issue_title: Support "ANY" HTTP method in API Gateway resources
issue_body:
Hey,

Currently it is not possible to call an API Gateway which has a resource with its HTTP method set to `ANY`. It is however possible to create such a resource in localstack. The  implemented behavior seems to be that localstack actually expects the HTTP method of incoming requests to be `ANY` instead of interpreting `ANY` as "any HTTP methods".

 I quickly investigated and the fix may be done either in [get_apigateway_integration](https://github.com/localstack/localstack/blob/master/localstack/utils/aws/aws_stack.py#L321-L335) or directly in [forward_request](https://github.com/localstack/localstack/blob/master/localstack/services/apigateway/apigateway_listener.py#L157-L169).

Thanks.

issue_title: Hitting maximum number of Redis clients.
issue_body:
In experiments where we've created several thousands of actors (each of which has a Redis client), Redis begins denying clients that try to connect because Redis has a maximum number of clients.

This was reported by @pcmoritz.

You can attempt to raise the maximum number of Redis clients from Python with something like the following.

```python
redis_client.config_set("maxclients", "90000")
```

However, this may raise an exception if the the limit on the number of open files is too low.

That can be raised with `ulimit -n 100000` or something like that, but to get this to work, you may need to do something like the following (pointed out by @atumanov).

```
sudo bash -c ' echo "$USER hard nofile 100000" >> /etc/security/limits.conf '
```

issue_title: Proper handling of updates
issue_body:
If anyone is interested in collaborating, please refer to the [better-updates](https://github.com/LonamiWebs/Telethon/tree/better-updates) branch. This is an open source project and personally updates don't bother me that much. If you really need updates, feel free to contribute to Telethon instead reinventing the wheel. I will happily merge.

Currently updates are not handled in their best way, duplicates may be received, we never call [`GetDifferenceRequest`](https://lonamiwebs.github.io/Telethon/methods/updates/get_difference.html), and the managing threads or multiple connections should in general be reworked to properly accommodate this feature.

issue_title: Option to prevent duplicate images from webcam when using ffmpeg Reader.get_next_data()?
issue_body:
Below is a feature proposal, but I would be happy to turn it into a pull request.

Problem
----------

As far as I can see, when used with a webcam stream, `FfmpegFormat.Reader.get_next_data()` always returns an `Image`, regardless of whether-or-not a _new_ image is actually available from the webcam. Thus, the image returned may be either new or a duplicate of the previous one. 

This is probably intentional, but, if the user is unaware and expects a kind of blocking behavior (as I was/did initially...), it may lead to unexpected results. 

For example, in the code below, without additional timing control, we will get many unnecessary calls to `update_screen` and `append_data`, with duplicate frames in the resulting video file (assuming the loop frequency is higher than the camera framerate).

    while True:
        new_image = reader.get_next_data()
        update_screen(new_image)
        writer.append_data(new_image)

To fix this, the user would need to check for duplicate images or implement some other form of timing control.

Relevance
-----------

On the one hand, I can see why such a check for duplicates may be considered an implementation detail, but, on the other hand, I guess there are very few use cases where the user will _not_ benefit from a built-in (but optional) check for duplicates.

Proposed solution
--------------------

A convenient way to implement a check for duplicates in the ffmpeg `Reader.get_next_data()` would be to provide the _option_ to return `None` in case the image is a duplicate of the previous image (e.g. the `only_new` argument below). A user can then time his/her loop using a simple truth test:
    
    new_image = reader.get_next_data(only_new=True)
    if new_image is not None:
        update_screen(new_image)
        writer.append_data(new_image)

Setting `only_new=False` by default would ensure backward compatibility.

To reduce the performance penalty resulting from the equality test, we can test a limited number of pixels, e.g. let `only_new` take a (percentage) value instead of a boolean.

Alternatives
-------------
Maybe similar functionality is already available but I haven't found it yet?

Possible alternatives would be:

- ignore this issue and just let the user deal with it
- let the `get_next_data()` method block until a new frame is available
- use an event-driven approach, e.g. a `data_available` event (not sure if this can be implemented via ffmpeg and/or subprocess)

issue_title: Allow developers to choose the return type ('json', 'xml') for services like "Relationship Extraction"
issue_body:
This is not an issue, I just wanted to bring this up as an enchancement: If you see the file "relationship_extraction_v1_beta.py", you can see that "'rt': 'xml'" is hardcoded.

This means that this service will ALWAYS return "XML" as its format. However, I prefer using JSON, and other developers have their own preferences, so I propose a solution: Just let the developer choose which Data Type they prefer! We can do this by adding "rt" or "return_type" as one of the parameters passed into the "extract" function of the class.

issue_title: Change in behaviour of neuron checking functions
issue_body:
The neuron checking functions were designed at a time when NeuroM was an SWC-only package. As such, it made sense to return IDs of points that caused failures. This was intended to help determined users to debug SWC morphology files. However, NeuroM now deals with HDF5 and (experimentally) with NeuriLucida .asc files, for which the concept of point ID is meaningless. 

I suggest changing the reporting of failures to either return nothing, or return a section ID, point ID pair. The latter can be used to debug interactively and programatically (but should not be persisted.)

Does this make sense? And if so, do we return nothing, or the ID pair?

issue_title: Error with redshift does_table_exist()
issue_body:
<!---
Currently, there are no strict procedures or guidelines for submitting issues.
In short, please just use common sense.

Common sense includes this at bare-minimum:

 * search for similar issues posted before creating a new issue.
 * Use markdown to format all code/logs. Issues which are hard to read
   when rendered on GitHub might be closed with a friendly reminder of this.

Also, add steps to reproduce the bug, if applicable. Sample code would be nice too :)

For more information on how to submit valuable contributions,
see https://opensource.guide/how-to-contribute/#how-to-submit-a-contribution
-->
Redshift schema and table names are case insensitive (`select * from TableTest;` == `select * from tabletest;` == `select * from TaBlEtEsT;`). However, both queries in [does_table_exist](https://github.com/spotify/luigi/blob/master/luigi/contrib/redshift.py#L327-L346) compare case sensitive table names.

These should be updated to case insensitive comparisons.

The `%s` string parameters need to be surrounded with `lower()`

It may be a few days before i can submit the PR, so feel free to beat me to it.

Thanks,

issue_title: Pre-hook is being called a bit later than what I'd have expected
issue_body:
## Background
I have all egress traffic blocked on my server. All scripts initiating outgoing connection must call the firewall API to request a shot-lived exception. This is done with a script `enable-outgoing-traffic.sh`. This seems to be a good case for post and pre hooks.

## Info
- certbot 0.19.0
- Installed with APT
- Running command `cerbot renew`
- Output:

```
Attempting to renew cert xxx) from /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/xxx.conf produced an unexpected error: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='acme-staging.api.letsencrypt.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /directory (Caused by NewConnectionError('<urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection object at 0x7fd7dc90ae10>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno -2] Name or service not known',)). Skipping.
All renewal attempts failed. The following certs could not be renewed:
  /etc/letsencrypt/live/xxx/fullchain.pem (failure)
```

## What happened
In `renew_cert()`, pre-hook is called from inside `_get_and_save_cert()`, which is happening after `_init_le_client()`, which seems to be contacting the ACME API to determine the account. Since no egress connections are allowed, Certbot fails.

## What I expected
I expected the pre-hook to be called right after Certbot determined that the cert is due for renewal.

## Possible fix
At least in my case, moving `hooks.pre_hook(config)` call to the [`if should_get_cert`](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/3087b436f3be0adbbb2800263288cd5aae452828/certbot/main.py#L696) block or even to [`renew_cert()`](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/3087b436f3be0adbbb2800263288cd5aae452828/certbot/main.py#L740) works to fix the issue for me.

## PR?
I've been considering making a PR with my changes, but I don't quite understand the big picture consequences of moving the pre-hook call to before the LE client initialisation. It might be that this is the intentional and, indeed, expected behaviour. Ideas?

To fix, I had to add group-write access to the socket, and put diamond in the ceph group

issue_title: Enable webpack code splitting with `import()`
issue_body:
## Summary

We use buble to do our code transpilation, in both good and bad ways it adheres very closely to ES2015 specifications, and throws errors if we try to use `import` (an ES2015 reserved word) in the way that Webpack needs.

This is not a huge deal, as we can still use the Webpack `require.ensure` code splitting, but it would be nice to have to make our asynchronous code splitting natively Promise driven.

There are two possible solutions:

- Try to make webpack look for something other than `import()` and use a different keyword for our asynchronous code splitting.
- Fork/modify Buble to allow the webpack `import()` syntax, and submit upstream.

The pre-trained shuffleNet ONNX model is here: https://github.com/onnx/models/tree/master/shufflenet 2

From the tutorial of the loading of ONNX model (https://mxnet.incubator.apache.org/tutorials/onnx/super_resolution.html?highlight=onnx), the simple model loading code is here:

import mxnet.contrib.onnx as onnx_mxnet
import json
def loadShuffleNet():
path = ‘/model/shufflenet/’
onnx_path = path + ‘model.onnx’
sym, arg_params, aux_params = onnx_mxnet.import_model(onnx_path)
print(sym.get_internals().list_outputs())

The error information is on the following:
—> 30 sym, arg_params, aux_params = onnx_mxnet.import_model(onnx_path)
31 print(sym.get_internals().list_outputs())
32 ln = ‘flatten0’

~/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/mxnet/contrib/onnx/_import/import_model.py in import_model(model_file)
51 # loads model file and returns ONNX protobuf object
52 model_proto = onnx.load(model_file)
—> 53 sym, arg_params, aux_params = graph.from_onnx(model_proto.graph)
54 return sym, arg_params, aux_params
55

~/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/mxnet/contrib/onnx/_import/import_onnx.py in from_onnx(self, graph)
112 onnx_attr = self._parse_attr(node.attribute)
113 inputs = [self._nodes[i] for i in node.input]
–> 114 mxnet_sym = self._convert_operator(node_name, op_name, onnx_attr, inputs)
115
116 for k, i in zip(list(node.output), ran
ge(len(mxnet_sym.list_outputs()))):

~/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/mxnet/contrib/onnx/_import/import_onnx.py in _convert_operator(self, node_name, op_name, attrs, inputs)
56 “”"
57 if op_name in convert_map:
—> 58 op_name, new_attrs, inputs = convert_map[op_name](attrs, inputs, self)
59 else:
60 raise NotImplementedError(“Operator {} not implemented.”.format(op_name))

~/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/mxnet/contrib/onnx/_import/op_translations.py in batch_norm(attrs, inputs, cls)
172
173 # in test mode “fix_gamma” should be unset.
–> 174 new_attrs[‘fix_gamma’] = 0 if new_attrs[‘fix_gamma’] == 1 else 1

KeyError: ‘fix_gamma’

After testing with all versions of the pre-trained shuffleNet, only the version 1.1 can be loaded successfully.

Is there anyone meeting this problem? Thank you.

Creating this issue on behalf of @jmnie 
https://discuss.mxnet.io/t/error-in-loading-pretrained-shufflenet-onnx-model/1520

issue_title: Optimizers memory usage
issue_body:
There's been few comments about memory consumption in the R-package. With large models, the memory footprint can rapidly burst the available memory unless a manual gc() is added in the training loop. 

The problematic appears to be the weights update performed by the optimizers: https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/R-package/R/model.R#L221

Memory behaves as if the temporary nd.arrays were kept in memory. 

Looked at how the python optimizers were designed and took a first attempt at using the `mx.nd.sgd.update` type of operators. 
It seemed to help, but looking at https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/python/mxnet/optimizer.py#L434, I confused at how the state of optimizers such as momentum or adam and cie is updated. I can't see where the staste is updated other than for the initialization. 

Also, tried to do a direct mutation of the weight using the out parameters of the `mx.nd.sgd.update` to point to the executor ref.arg.arrays, but it got the following message after a first update: 

```
 Error in mx.nd.sgd.update(weight = weight, out = out, grad = grad, lr = learning.rate,  : 
  ./ndarray.h:87: RCheck failed: ptr_->writable && !ptr_->moved Passing a read only NDArray to mutate function 
```
I'm still missing some pieces to figure out a proper way of fixing the memory consumtion of the optimizers. Any help would be much welcome!

issue_body:
